In the investigation it was used to view incoming call activity and diagnostics (showing no incoming call entries for the affected numbers), inspect Direct Routing configuration and SBC health (SBC reported Online and call signaling was present), and verify user licensing and voice-routing policy assignments. The console therefore provided evidence that signaling paths were intact while RTP media was not bidirectional for the impacted calls.

title: SBC (SESSION BORDER CONTROLLER)
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: SBC (SESSION BORDER CONTROLLER): The SBC is the on‑premises (or hosted) SIP/Direct Routing gateway that terminates carrier calls and handles SIP signaling and media for external calls to Microsoft Teams. The carrier confirmed delivery of calls to the SBC. The SBC hosts translation tables and applies routing/translation rules for number normalization and call routing before forwarding calls to Teams. Logs show split behavior by number: for functioning numbers the SBC accepted the incoming INVITE and forwarded the call to Teams, while for affected numbers the SBC returned/answered with a "404 Not Found" SIP response. In other call traces the SBC completed SIP INVITE/200 OK call establishment but then issued a BYE after approximately 30 seconds. During troubleshooting, SBC session timers and temporary parameter adjustments were exercised to reproduce the short‑call termination and to mitigate the problem.

title: CARRIER / PROVIDER
description: External telephony/carrier provider who delivers PSTN calls to the customer's SBC. Carrier reported that they delivered calls correctly to the SBC and saw no routing errors on their side; helped rule out upstream delivery issues.

title: SBC TRANSLATION TABLE
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Configuration artifact on the SBC containing number translation entries / mapping and normalization rules. Investigation found new number range entries were missing from the translation table, causing INVITEs for that range to be mis-answered (404) and not forwarded to Teams.

title: ROUTING AND TRANSLATION RULES
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Set of SBC and Direct Routing configuration rules that normalize inbound numbers (E.164), translate number formats and direct signaling to Teams voice routing policies. These rules initially only covered the old number range and were later extended to include the new range per the architecture.

title: VOICE ROUTING POLICIES
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Policies that determine how normalized/translated inbound numbers are routed to Teams users and voicemail/auto attendants. Part of the Direct Routing architecture referenced in the Blueprint and checked during the internal review.

title: E.164 NORMALIZATION
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Number normalization standard/practice (E.164) applied by translation rules on the SBC and routing policies to ensure provider numbers map correctly to internal routing/extension formats; omission of correct normalization for the new range contributed to calls not being routed.

title: SBC LOGS
description: SBC LOGS: Session Border Controller log records examined during troubleshooting. The logs showed successful SIP call setup in some cases (INVITE, 200 OK, call established) followed by a BYE sent by the SBC at approximately 30 seconds with no obvious SIP error code in the teardown. Separately, session logs produced by the SBC during the investigation revealed differing SIP behavior between functioning and affected numbers: for working numbers the SBC accepted and forwarded the INVITE, while for affected numbers the INVITE was effectively answered with a 404 Not Found. Analysis of these logs was a primary diagnostic step and localized the fault to SBC translation/routing rather than to an explicit SIP protocol error.

title: NEW NUMBER RANGE
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: The new telephone number range provisioned by the carrier and partially migrated by the customer the week prior to the issue. The affected example numbers (+41 44 785 23 10/11) belong to this new range; the new range was not present in the SBC translation table/routing rules, which caused calls in this range to be mis-answered (404) and not forwarded to Teams.

title: OLD NUMBER RANGE
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: The previously configured phone number range(s) that were already included in the SBC translation/routing rules. Investigation found routing rules covered only the old range, causing the newer range to be excluded until rules were updated.

title: SIP TRUNK
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: The SIP trunk/link used by the carrier to deliver inbound PSTN calls to the customer's SBC. The carrier reported that calls were delivered correctly to the SIP trunk/SBC, which helped isolate the fault to SBC-side handling rather than transport delivery.

title: TEMPORARY TRANSLATION RULE
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: A temporary modification applied to the SBC translation/normalization rules during testing that added the new number range to the translation table. This temporary rule was used to verify that including the new range corrected signaling and call delivery before the permanent architectural update was applied; the temporary rule produced successful test calls when active and the issue was reproducible when it was reverted.

title: INTERNAL DOCUMENTATION
description: Customer/internal operational documentation (separate from the official "Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3") that had not been updated to reflect the newly assigned number range and migration. The outdated internal docs contributed to the oversight where the SBC translation table was not updated in time.

title: TI-1609823
description: Support incident opened 03.09.25 (Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed). Company: Customer Company 52 AG. Contact: Contact Person 21. Owner/assignee: Employee 39. Origin: Email. Created: 03.09.25 09:08. Closed: 12.09.25 15:40. Closure summary: Analysis completed; routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture and external dialing restored. Ticket records investigation steps, Teams Admin Center analysis, internal reviews, tests, coordination with the Identity team, blueprint reference (Version 1.3), provisioning script changes, and final policy assignment correction.

title: CONTACT PERSON 21
description: Customer contact who reported the issue (email on 03.09.25) and provided follow-up information about scope and organizational changes; recipient of closure communication.

title: EMPLOYEE 39
description: Support engineer/owner assigned to TI-1609823 who coordinated analysis, testing, communications, and the final configuration changes that restored external calling.

title: CALLING PLAN
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Microsoft Calling Plan product/feature; noted as not active for the affected users (i.e., Direct Routing was used instead of Calling Plan).

title: VOICE ROUTING POLICY: GLOBAL
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Voice routing policy that affected users were assigned to (observed value). Investigation found that the "Global" policy did not reference any PSTN Usage, so it provided no external routing path for Direct Routing-enabled users, causing external calls to fail.

title: VOICE ROUTING POLICY: DR-STANDARD
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Working voice routing policy observed on other users. DR-Standard includes PSTN Usage "External-Route" and allows external dialing for Direct Routing users; temporarily assigning DR-Standard to an affected user restored external calling in tests.

title: VOICE ROUTING POLICY: DR-SALES
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: A policy referenced in comparisons/expectations (noted that affected users had "Global" and not "DR-Sales"). Represents the intended/dedicated routing policy for Sales/Direct Routing users in the environment or a named variant mentioned during analysis.

title: PSTN USAGE: EXTERNAL-ROUTE
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: A PSTN Usage object referenced in routing policy DR-Standard. Provides the external route to the PSTN for voice calls (presence in a voice routing policy enables outbound PSTN dialing).

title: SALES TEAM
description: SALES TEAM (Customer Company 52 AG) is an organizational team of roughly 12 employees. A subset of about six users—recently reassigned into the new department "Enterprise Sales"—were affected by an issue where, while using older Outlook clients, outgoing mail became stuck in the Outbox. Remediation was targeted at these affected users by selectively enabling SMTP AUTH.

title: ENTERPRISE-SALES (AZURE AD GROUP)
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: New Azure AD group created to reflect the organizational reassignment ("Enterprise-Sales"). Membership in this group triggered automated provisioning logic that assigned the "Global" voice routing policy by default.

title: AZURE AD
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Azure Active Directory tenant/service used for user/group management in the customer's environment. New Azure AD groups were created during the reorganization; group membership drives automated provisioning that assigns Teams voice routing policies.

title: PROVISIONING SCRIPT
description: Automated provisioning script/logic that assigns voice routing policies when users become members of certain Azure AD groups. A recent change to this script caused new Enterprise-Sales group members to be assigned the "Global" policy by default; script/logic was later modified to correct policy assignment.

title: BLUEPRINT – TEAMS VOICE ROUTING & POLICY ASSIGNMENT – VERSION 1.3
description: Internal architecture/blueprint document that specifies voice routing and policy assignment standards. It states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy. The investigation referenced this blueprint as the source of expected policy assignments.

title: IDENTITY TEAM
description: Internal team responsible for identity/provisioning. Coordinated changes with the support team; the provisioning script that assigned the wrong policy was modified two weeks prior and adjustments were coordinated with this team.

title: AFFECTED USERS (ABOUT 6 USERS)
description: Group representing the ~6 Sales users who could not place external calls. They had Direct Routing configured but were assigned the "Global" Voice Routing Policy lacking PSTN Usage, which caused external calls to fail until policy assignment was corrected.

title: ENTERPRISE SALES
description: Organizational department (newly created) to which the affected Sales users were reassigned; represents the business unit context for the Azure AD group "Enterprise-Sales" and is the target of the provisioning/policy mapping.

title: TEAMS VOICE ARCHITECTURE
description: Architectural guidance for Teams voice routing and policy assignment referenced in the ticket closure and used to validate/adjust routing configuration; aligns with and informs the "Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3".

title: AZURE AD GROUPS
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: AZURE AD GROUPS are Azure AD group objects created during the reorganization (for example, Enterprise-Sales). They are authoritative identity constructs whose membership is consumed by provisioning logic and downstream systems to assign voice routing policies and other policy-based controls. Separately, dedicated Azure AD groups are used to assign SharePoint permissions; the governing document requires permissions to be granted exclusively via these dedicated groups and specifies group creation, naming conventions, and lifecycle (management and deprovisioning) requirements. In short, these groups serve as the single-source group-based mechanism for both voice routing policy assignment and SharePoint permissioning, governed by documented creation, naming, and lifecycle rules.

title: BLUEPRINT: IDENTITY & AUTHENTICATION (AD FS / ENTRA ID INTEGRATION)
description: A blueprint document (Version 0.1, 11.02.2026) describing the AD FS / Microsoft Entra ID integration architecture, decision trees, use cases, best practices, licensing guidance and troubleshooting steps for identity and authentication. Covers token formats (SAML, JWT/OIDC), MFA integration, client registration parameters (Client ID, Redirect URI), separation of browser vs non-browser flows, documentation and review practices for client registrations and certificates. Serves as the authoritative reference for diagnosing authentication issues and escalation to Identity/Architecture teams.

title: AD FS
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Active Directory Federation Services: on-premises identity provider described as the central authentication/authorization service in the blueprint. Issues and client registration parameters in AD FS determine whether it issues SAML or JWT/OIDC tokens, how MFA is applied (directly or delegated), and how integrations and applications authenticate. AD FS is the primary subject for configuration, certificate management, client registrations (Client ID, Redirect URI), and troubleshooting steps in the document.

title: MICROSOFT ENTRA ID
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD): cloud identity platform referenced as a complementary/combined component in hybrid architectures. Used in conjunction with AD FS for token issuance, licensing considerations (Free/P1/P2 tiers), Conditional Access and optional cloud MFA capabilities. The blueprint covers when Entra ID features may be required and how it interoperates with AD FS.

title: JWT / OPENID CONNECT / OAUTH2
description: JSON Web Token (JWT) and the OpenID Connect / OAuth2 flows: token format and protocol family that AD FS can issue when EnableJWT/OpenID Connect style client registrations are used. JWT tokens have signatures that target applications must validate; incompatibility or lack of validation support in an application can cause non-browser integrations to fail even when browser logins succeed.

title: SAML
description: SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) is a token/assertion format used in federated authentication as an alternative to JSON Web Tokens (JWT). It can be issued or handled by federation services such as ADFS and is typically chosen for specific applications and integrations whose target systems must be compatible with SAML assertions. The blueprint recommends deliberately choosing SAML or JWT on a per-application basis and checking SAML configuration when troubleshooting failures in SAML-based applications.

title: MULTI-FACTOR AUTHENTICATION (MFA)
description: MULTI-FACTOR AUTHENTICATION (MFA): The organization enforces MFA as a primary authentication control, although a specific service account was temporarily excluded from the MFA requirement while identified login issues were investigated and remediated for the NDES connector. The MFA blueprint documents two integration approaches: enforcing MFA directly via AD FS or delegating MFA to external claims providers. It explains that delegating MFA changes where and how multifactor checks are evaluated and logged, and can alter authentication flows—potentially causing failures or degraded behavior for non‑browser clients, service-account integrations, or other noninteractive SCEP/NDES scenarios. The temporary exclusion of the service account was limited to remediation activities and reflects the practical impact these integration choices have on operational reliability, logging visibility, and security posture.

title: CLAIMS PROVIDERS
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: CLAIMS PROVIDERS are the external or additional identity/MFA providers configured in Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS) to which AD FS can delegate authentication or multifactor authentication (MFA). When AD FS is configured to forward MFA requests (for example via RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders or RequestMFA-type behavior), those claims providers perform the MFA operations and thus become part of the authentication flow. Because MFA is handled externally in this way, authentication flows become more complex and can require special handling or capabilities in the target application (such as support for federated/claims-based authentication, explicit MFA claim handling, claim transformations, error/timeout handling, and appropriate logging and trust relationships). Operational impacts include added dependency on the availability and behavior of the external providers, additional configuration for trust and attribute mapping, and potential implications for user experience and auditability.

title: BROWSER-BASED LOGINS (WEB INTERFACES)
description: Browser-based authentication flows (web logins) described as one class of authentication where SSO and interactive MFA typically work; blueprint highlights that browser-based logins may succeed while non-browser integrations fail due to differences in client parameters and token formats.

title: NON-BROWSER-BASED LOGINS (SERVICES, INTEGRATIONS, APIS)
description: Non-interactive authentication flows used by services, background processes, APIs and integrations. These flows often rely on client registrations (OIDC/OAuth2 or SAML) and explicit token format support (JWT vs SAML). The blueprint instructs specific checks for these flows when browser logins succeed but integrations fail.

title: APPLICATIONS / INTEGRATIONS / APIS
description: Target applications and integrations that consume tokens from AD FS / Entra ID (web apps, APIs, background services). These components must support the token format issued and be configured with correct client registration parameters; they are the entities that experience authentication failures described in the decision tree use cases.

title: CLIENT REGISTRATION PARAMETERS (CLIENT ID, REDIRECT URI)
description: Configuration artifacts for registered clients in AD FS/Entra ID (Client ID, Redirect URI and other client flags). The blueprint emphasizes documenting these registrations and verifying correctness as a key troubleshooting and governance item; incorrect client registration parameters are a common root cause of application authentication failures.

title: CERTIFICATES & TOKEN SIGNATURES
description: Certificates and token signing keys used by AD FS (and Entra ID) to sign tokens. The blueprint recommends reviewing certificates, token signatures, expiry and time skew as part of troubleshooting intermittent or user-specific authentication errors; certificate management is called out as a recurring best-practice area.

title: LOGGING AND MONITORING SYSTEMS
description: LOGGING AND MONITORING SYSTEMS: Consulting Sample Company (and its support organisation) uses logging, monitoring and audit systems to validate remediation and to diagnose complex authentication and synchronization issues. Beginning 15.04.25, the environment was actively monitored post‑remediation to observe access stability, confirm that group consolidation and a full Azure AD Connect synchronization resolved prior inconsistencies, and verify that no further incidents occurred. The blueprint cites these systems as essential for troubleshooting flows where MFA is delegated to external claims providers and for tracing token issuance, client errors, and differences between browser and non‑browser authentication paths. Recommended practices include capturing Azure AD sign‑in and sync logs, claims provider and token issuance events, client error codes and correlation IDs, and change/audit trails for group membership and directory synchronization operations; correlating timestamps across systems; and using alerts and dashboards to detect sync failures or authentication anomalies. Together these capabilities provide the auditability and diagnostic evidence required for incident confirmation, support handover, and ongoing assurance of authentication and directory health.

title: IDENTITY TEAM / ARCHITECTURE TEAM
description: Identity or Architecture team: escalation target when the decision tree does not resolve an issue. Responsible for deeper analysis of AD FS configuration, client registrations, certificates, and cross-system integrations. The blueprint explicitly instructs escalation to these teams when parameter adjustments don't resolve the problem.

title: APPLICATION OWNER
description: Application owner/stakeholder: the responsible person or team for a specific application or integration. The blueprint instructs clarifying the authentication protocol and requirements with the application owner when the mechanism is unknown or when application-specific configuration must be validated.

title: WINDOWS SERVER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Windows Server: the server operating system platform required to host and operate AD FS. The blueprint explicitly notes that a Windows Server license is required for operating AD FS and that AD FS runs in a Windows Server environment, impacting deployment, patching, and certificate management.

title: CONDITIONAL ACCESS
description: CONDITIONAL ACCESS: Azure AD (Microsoft Entra ID) Conditional Access is an Entra ID policy capability used to enforce access controls — for example requiring MFA, checking device compliance or state, and evaluating sign‑in conditions — and is referenced in licensing guidance/blueprints as a licensing‑dependent feature (typically requiring Microsoft Entra ID P1/P2). In the incident described, the organization's Conditional Access policies were evaluated and reported as successful for the affected sign‑ins (no blocking rule was observed); therefore Conditional Access was determined not to be the cause of the access denial.

title: LICENSING (MICROSOFT ENTRA ID TIERS: FREE / P1 / P2)
description: Licensing guidance excerpt in the blueprint describing required licenses and tiers for features: Microsoft Entra ID Free / P1 / P2 (noting that features such as Conditional Access or advanced MFA may require P1/P2) and the Windows Server license requirement for AD FS. This section informs design and feature availability decisions.

title: DECISION TREE – AUTHENTICATION ISSUES
description: The Decision Tree section (Questions 1–10) providing a structured troubleshooting flow for authentication issues (distinguishing browser vs non-browser logins, identifying protocols OIDC/JWT vs SAML, checking client registration parameters, MFA delegation checks, certificate/time checks, and escalation criteria). Used operationally to triage and escalate identity incidents.

title: USE CASES (LOGIN WORKS IN BROWSER / CONFIG CHANGES / INTERMITTENT / ESCALATION)
description: The Use Cases section listing common scenarios (e.g., login works in browser but not in application; authentication errors after config changes; intermittent/user-specific errors; escalation decision criteria). These cases provide concrete examples and remediation paths tied to the decision tree and troubleshooting guidance.

title: TI-1385257
description: Support ticket opened 26.11.24 (Ticket name: KF11: Customer company 11CE TRAIN: Connection error from DevOps and FO). Source: Email. Priority: Normal. Contact: Employee 14 (reporter) and Employee 16 (initial contact). Owner(s)/assignees: Employee 24 (assigned after transfers). Status timeline: Created 26.11.24, set In Progress 28.11.24, reopened request 09.12.24, closed 18.12.24. Closure note: "Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment". Ticket contains portal notes, assignment transfers, and email activities documenting investigation and remediation.

title: CUSTOMER COMPANY 11AG
description: Customer organization listed in the ticket metadata as the company for which TI-1385257 was recorded. The affected environment and users belong to this organization.

title: CUSTOMER COMPANY 11CE TRAIN
description: Referenced environment/tenant/system context in the ticket subject ("Customer company 11CE TRAIN") and the owner/context of the TRAIN CE server that experienced connectivity/authentication issues.

title: EMPLOYEE 24
description: EMPLOYEE 24 was assigned ownership of the ticket after it was transferred from Admin. The assignment occurred on 27.11.24, and EMPLOYEE 24 is recorded as the ticket owner during subsequent workflow transitions, serving as the later assignee/owner in the ticket workflow.

title: EMPLOYEE 21
description: EMPLOYEE 21 is a support/system engineer who served as the assigned ticket owner and lead analyst across multiple related incidents (including TI-1571046). They performed initial remote troubleshooting (including testing print‑server behavior), coordinated remediation activities with the Network Team, and managed ticket remediation and closure. On 18.12.24 they authored the documented ADFS client configuration notes and provided the root‑cause analysis identifying misconfigured ADFS client registration parameters (EnableJWT and RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders); they implemented and documented the parameter changes intended to prevent further JWT/MFA login failures. While handling TI-1571046 they reviewed Conditional Access and sign‑in logs, ran token analysis, adjusted a Conditional Access policy (excluding a SharePoint project folder from a Finance‑specific CA rule), re‑authenticated affected users, triggered device synchronization, monitored results, and closed the ticket. Later (took over 06.06.25) in their System Engineer capacity they performed WAF analysis, identified TLS inspection as the cause of 502 errors when enabled, compared the WAF configuration to the internal blueprint, and helped apply configuration adjustments that restored external access.

title: EMPLOYEE 7
description: Recipient/stakeholder referenced in the detailed analysis communication about TRAIN-CE/FO integration and MFA; addressed by Employee 21 in the explanatory note.

title: KF11\\001-SVC-AXAVAOBT-TRA
description: KF11\\001-SVC-AXAVAOBT-TRA (also referenced in portal notes as KF11\\001-svc-axavaobt-tra) is a service account identified in ticket portal entries as "the account currently configured" for the affected integration. Portal notes mark this account as a user/account of interest and a likely suspect in the reported connectivity/authentication problems, and investigators have specifically queried whether its password or credentials were changed. The account name format indicates a KF11\\001 domain/context prefix and the service account identifier (SVC-AXAVAOBT-TRA), and it should be treated as a primary focus for troubleshooting authentication and configuration checks.

title: TRAIN CE SERVER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: The application/server environment (TRAIN CE) experiencing connectivity and authentication errors. A restart of the application server was performed as a remediation attempt but did not resolve the problem. The Train CE integration relies on ADFS for authentication.

title: APPLICATION SERVER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: APPLICATION SERVER: an internal backend application server process/component that hosts the TRAIN CE application and its portal. A restart of this server was attempted in the referenced ticket. The server is reachable only from the internal network and responded successfully in internal tests; the load balancer reported the backend as healthy via its health checks. The application expects the upstream proxy/load balancer to provide an X-Forwarded-Proto: https header (i.e., it relies on the upstream to indicate HTTPS when TLS is terminated/offloaded). Given the successful internal responses and healthy LB checks, any externally visible issues are more likely related to upstream TLS termination or the X-Forwarded-Proto header not being set/corrupted rather than a failed application process on the APPLICATION SERVER itself.

title: TOOLBOX

title: ENABLEJWT
description: ADFS client registration parameter which, when enabled, causes ADFS to issue JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) during authentication instead of other formats like SAML. If the target application does not support or cannot validate JWTs, enabling this may cause authentication failures.

title: REQUESTMFAFROMCLAIMSPROVIDERS
description: REQUESTMFAFROMCLAIMSPROVIDERS is an ADFS client registration parameter that, when enabled, forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers—effectively delegating the execution of multifactor authentication to external identity providers. Enabling it changes the authentication flow by introducing external MFA orchestration, which increases operational and diagnostic complexity and can affect logging and auditing. It can also cause interoperability problems when a relying party or downstream system does not expect or cannot process external MFA claims.

title: JWT
description: JWT (JSON Web Token) — referenced as a token type that ADFS (Active Directory Federation Services) may issue when the EnableJWT option is enabled or when certain client parameters are set. When ADFS issues JWTs, the target application must be able to accept the JWT format and perform signature validation of the token; otherwise authentication or login will fail. Enabling JWT issuance in ADFS can therefore cause login failures for downstream systems not designed to handle JWTs.

title: LOGGING AND AUDITING
description: Conceptual entity referenced in the analysis noting that forwarding MFA to claims providers increases complexity for logging, monitoring, and audit trails — implying impacts to system logs and audit processes.

title: ADFS CONFIGURATION DOCUMENTATION

title: SHAREPOINT
description: SHAREPOINT: A Microsoft SharePoint site/library environment contained folders (notably /Projects/ERP_Upgrade/Finance and /Projects/Compliance_Review_2025) where unique permissions had been applied, a folder was renamed, and later a Sensitivity Label with encryption was applied. Although SharePoint ACLs reported access as allowed, some users were blocked because the encryption key/label permissions (separate from the ACL) prevented decryption depending on the user’s token state. Access behavior varied — users with stale or cached tokens (or tokens evaluated differently by Conditional Access) could be denied even when ACLs showed permission, because SharePoint’s enforcement combined ACLs, label-based encryption key permissions, and Conditional Access/token claims. In addition, the affected /Projects/Compliance_Review_2025 folder used group-based permissions and SharePoint evaluates group membership by Azure AD object ID; that made access sensitive to which group object was referenced (a cloud-created group versus an on-premises-synchronized group), so mismatched group object IDs or membership state contributed to inconsistent access.

title: CUSTOMER PORTAL (PORTAL.KF40.CH)
description: The CUSTOMER PORTAL (portal.kf40.ch) is a public-facing web application registered as a subscriber of the Web Publishing architecture and designated to be published using the Portal‑App‑Template with WAF/reverse‑proxy perimeter controls. An incident affecting the FQDN https://portal.kf40.ch caused external requests to return 502 Bad Gateway while internal access continued to succeed. Troubleshooting revealed the backend requires the X-Forwarded-Proto: https header because TLS is offloaded at the perimeter and the portal has a documented special header‑forwarding requirement for TLS offloading. The WAF/reverse‑proxy was not initially forwarding that header; updating its configuration per the Portal‑App‑Template/blueprint to forward X-Forwarded-Proto restored full external functionality.

title: PORTAL.KF40.CH
description: The portal FQDN used for external access. External DNS resolution was tested and reported as correct. Tests via FQDN produced 502 while direct IP to reverse proxy worked, indicating DNS resolution was not the root cause but the name-based path exposed WAF/header handling issues.

title: REVERSE PROXY / WAF
description: The external reverse proxy / web application firewall protecting and publishing the portal. Attributes observed: TLS inspection (TLS termination/inspection) enabled, HTTP/2 enabled, active policy applied, inspection template changed after security patch (Default-Strict loaded instead of Portal-App-Template), header rewriting/profile differences, and missing custom header rewrite rule. Temporarily disabling TLS inspection allowed successful login; re-enabling it caused 502, indicating inspection removed/failed to forward the X-Forwarded-Proto header required by the backend. TLS termination and header rewrite behavior must follow the "Secure Web Publishing" blueprint.

title: FIREWALL
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: FIREWALL — Multiple network and perimeter firewalls across sites (an edge/location firewall at the Zurich site, a network firewall at the Basel site, and the perimeter firewall) were analyzed by the Network Team during a weekend incident and follow-up troubleshooting. At Zurich an emergency security‑hardening change deployed over the weekend introduced a new outbound restriction that blocked the media (RTP) ports from clients to the Session Border Controller (SBC), which produced no audio for users calling external parties. At Basel the team found standard port configuration and no blocked or closed ports; helpdesk verification showed an allowed port 443 and NAT entries that appeared correct. Investigation also examined firewall/NAT interaction and state timeout behavior in relation to the Voice VLAN NAT policy. Separately, the perimeter firewall received a security patch/upgrade over the same weekend that (despite logs not showing obvious drops) overwrote or altered templates/policies and caused configuration drift impacting reverse‑proxy/WAF behavior. In addition, firewall rules that previously governed external FTP access were reviewed and a related policy was later removed during decommissioning. In summary, the outages and functional regressions were caused by recent rule/template changes and a patch-induced configuration drift rather than evident port closures in Basel firewall logs, with NAT/state timeout interactions contributing to the voice impact.

title: NAT
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Network address translation configuration used for inbound external traffic to the published service. Helpdesk confirmed NAT configuration was correct during initial checks and that port 443 rules existed.

title: LOAD BALANCER
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Load balancer in front of backend servers; reported backend marked as "Healthy" during checks. Tests showed backend reachable internally and health checks passed, indicating the backend itself was serviceable and the error was on the publishing/proxy/inspection path.

title: TLS CERTIFICATE
description: TLS CERTIFICATE: A new TLS certificate was recently installed on the publishing stack over the weekend (the customer reported the certificate). Helpdesk validated the certificate’s CN, SAN entries and trust chain with no obvious errors. Although the certificate change was noted temporally near a 502 incident, diagnostic tests showed the certificate validity and chain were not the direct cause of the 502. Separately, TLS certificate(s) on the Exchange hybrid infrastructure were also inspected during hybrid connectivity analysis to ensure secure transport (Send Connector/TLS) between on‑premises and cloud environments; these checks were part of the standard checklist and likewise were not identified as the root cause.

title: BLUEPRINT: SECURE WEB PUBLISHING – KF40 – VERSION 3.2
description: Internal architectural specification describing correct secure web publishing configuration for the portal. Key guidance: TLS termination must occur on WAF, backend expects HTTPS signaling, custom header rewrite rule required for X-Forwarded-Proto, inspection policy must not remove HTTP headers, and a specific exception/template defined for the portal application. The team compared actual config to this blueprint and applied adjustments per its recommendations.

title: MONITORING
description: Monitoring systems used to verify service stability post-remediation; external access tested successfully and monitoring held stable for 24 hours before ticket closure. Helpdesk and load balancer logs were used during troubleshooting, although no explicit blocking entries were observed in firewall logs.

title: DEFAULT-STRICT
description: Inspection/template profile that was loaded by default after the firewall/security patch (reloaded default profile). Ticket notes that "Default-Strict" became active after the patch and replaced the custom Portal-App-Template, contributing to header removal and 502 errors.

title: PORTAL-APP-TEMPLATE
description: PORTAL-APP-TEMPLATE is a custom WAF/inspection and reverse-proxy template mandated by the portal blueprint and portal publishing guidance for production portal applications. It encodes the documented HTTP profile and header-rewrite rules required for portal behavior—including header rewrite rules, preservation of HTTPS semantics, HTTP/2 compatibility settings, session handling, and specific adjustments to support authentication flows—so that portal authentication and session behavior function correctly behind the WAF. The blueprint explicitly requires use of PORTAL-APP-TEMPLATE for productive portals and prohibits using other templates (for example, Default-Strict). A recent ticket documented that the template had been rendered inactive by a patch and needed to be restored to reinstate the required HTTP profile and header-rewrite rules.

title: CUSTOM HEADER REWRITE RULE
description: A required custom rewrite rule on the reverse proxy/WAF to forward or rewrite headers (notably X-Forwarded-Proto) to the backend when TLS is offloaded/inspected. The rule was missing after the security patch/template reload and its absence caused the backend to receive requests without the expected HTTPS signaling.

title: TLS INSPECTION
description: TLS inspection/termination behavior on the WAF/reverse proxy. Ticket tests showed that temporarily disabling TLS inspection allowed external logins to succeed; re-enabling inspection reproduced 502, indicating inspection was removing or failing to forward required headers unless correctly configured per the blueprint.

title: TLS TERMINATION
description: The TLS termination function (where TLS is ended on the WAF/reverse proxy and plaintext forwarded to backend) referenced by the blueprint as the required deployment model ("TLS termination must occur on WAF"). Correct TLS termination behavior (with header forwarding) was necessary for the portal to work when the WAF inspects TLS.

title: HTTP PROFILE
description: HTTP profile/settings applied on the WAF that control header handling and protocol behaviors. Ticket notes the HTTP profile in use did not match the documented/versioned profile required by the blueprint, contributing to missing header forwarding and 502 responses.

title: DMS
description: Document Management System used to load the blueprint document ("Blueprint: Secure Web Publishing – KF40 – Version 3.2"). The team retrieved the blueprint from DMS to compare and align WAF configuration with documented architectural specifications.

title: BACKEND SERVER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)

title: SIP MESSAGES (INVITE / 200 OK / BYE)
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: SIP signaling elements observed in the call flow: successful INVITE and 200 OK to establish the call, and a BYE originating from the SBC at ~30 seconds that terminated calls. Session-Expires / session timer headers were reviewed as part of SIP analysis.

title: SESSION TIMER (SESSION-EXPIRES HEADER)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)

title: VOICE VLAN (NEW "VOICE" VLAN)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)

title: NAT POLICY / NAT SESSION TIMEOUT (UDP SESSION TIMEOUT)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: NAT configuration applied to the new Voice VLAN; UDP/NAT session timeout was configured to 30 seconds (causing session state to expire prematurely). Adjusted to align with architecture (e.g., 180s) to restore session persistence and call stability.

title: DIRECT ROUTING LOGS
description: Logs and diagnostics from the Teams Direct Routing environment analyzed by support: signaling and session behavior (SIP transaction success, session timers) were reviewed to identify why calls terminated after ~30 seconds.

title: BLUEPRINT – MICROSOFT TEAMS DIRECT ROUTING & SESSION HANDLING – VERSION 2.1
description: Internal architecture/blueprint document referenced during analysis. Contains guidance on Direct Routing session handling, NAT session persistence, and recommended session timer/NAT timeout configuration. Used as the basis for the final configuration change.

title: TEMPORARY SBC PARAMETER ADJUSTMENT
description: Temporary changes applied to SBC session parameters during testing to confirm that altering session handling could prevent the 30-second disconnect; reverting reproduced the problem. Used to validate the root cause before permanent change.

title: TEST CALLS (TO MOBILE PHONE)
description: Calls executed during troubleshooting (external calls to mobile numbers) used to reproduce the disconnection at ~29–31 seconds and to validate fixes after configuration changes.

title: END USER DEVICES (DOCKING STATION / LAN CABLE / OTHER DEVICE)
description: Endpoint hardware used in troubleshooting: tests with different user devices and replacing docking station with a LAN cable showed the behavior persisted, indicating the issue was network/session related rather than local device-specific.

title: MOBILE PHONE
description: External PSTN/mobile endpoint used as call target during tests to reproduce the drop after ~30 seconds.

title: NAT SESSION PERSISTENCE (CONCEPT)
description: Concept/behavior describing how NAT keeps UDP sessions alive; referenced by the blueprint and the internal analysis as the mechanism that must be aligned (timeouts, keepalives) to avoid premature termination of SIP/RTP flows.

title: BYE FROM SBC
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: The SIP BYE message originating from the Session Border Controller which terminated the external calls at ~29–31 seconds; observed in SBC logs as the immediate cause of call teardown without an explicit error code.

title: VOIP TRAFFIC
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Voice-over-IP traffic class carried on the new "Voice" VLAN at the Basel site; subject to distinct NAT policy and UDP session timeout behavior that affected SIP/RTP session persistence.

title: STANDARD PORT CONFIGURATION
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Reference to the firewall/network port ruleset described as "standard configuration" (ports open as expected) which was validated during firewall analysis; indicates that port blocking was not the cause and points to NAT/session timeout behavior instead.

title: CALL ENDED CLIENT MESSAGE
description: Teams client UI indication ("Call Ended") shown to users when the external call disconnected at ~30 seconds; used as an observable symptom in tests and reproduced across endpoints.

title: WEB PUBLISHING BLUEPRINT - CUSTOMER COMPANY 40
description: Document that defines the secure publication architecture for Customer Company 40's web-based applications. Describes a perimeter WAF/Reverse Proxy design with TLS termination at the perimeter, backend servers in a separate server VLAN, preservation of HTTPS semantics, standardized templates (Portal-App-Template) for portal applications, header rewrite rules, session handling, load balancing, validation steps after security/firmware patches, and a decision tree for troubleshooting external reachability, 502/503 errors, redirect loops, certificate/patch checks, and TLS inspection verification. Mandates that WAF profiles, TLS policies, and reverse proxy rules be validated against this blueprint for changes.

title: CUSTOMER COMPANY 40
description: The customer organization and owner/context for which the Web Publishing Blueprint is defined; hosts published services such as portal.kf40.ch, partner access, and internal web services with external interfaces.

title: WEB APPLICATION FIREWALL (WAF)
description: Perimeter security device/concept performing TLS termination (TLS offloading), request inspection, header rewrite, and load balancing for published portal applications. WAF enforces templates (Portal-App-Template) and inspection policies, must preserve or inject X-Forwarded headers to maintain HTTPS semantics toward backends, and must not remove/overwrite headers or custom rewrite rules. WAF templates and profiles must be validated after security patches.

title: REVERSE PROXY LAYER
description: Forwarding/proxy layer that receives external requests at the perimeter and forwards them to backend application servers; responsibilities include host header handling, session persistence, session handling, and preserving/requesting HTTPS semantics for backend servers. Operates in conjunction with the WAF and uses configured templates and rewrite rules.

title: BACKEND APPLICATION SERVER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Internal servers hosting portal, partner, or internal web service applications. Located in a separate server VLAN with no direct Internet access, expect HTTPS semantics from the perimeter layer (even if transport between WAF and backend uses HTTP), and require internal authentication. Their correct operation depends on the WAF/reverse proxy preserving or providing original-request context via headers.

title: SERVER VLAN
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Isolated network segment where backend application servers reside. Designed to prevent direct internet exposure of servers; network isolation enforces the "No direct publication of internal servers" principle from the blueprint.

title: TLS TERMINATION (TLS OFFLOADING)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Perimeter-level process where TLS is terminated on the WAF/Reverse Proxy so that inspection and protection can occur at the perimeter. After termination, communication to backends is performed over HTTP by default, requiring header-based preservation of original HTTPS semantics.

title: X-FORWARDED HEADERS (X-FORWARDED-PROTO, X-FORWARDED-HOST)
description: HTTP headers that must be set by the perimeter (WAF/Reverse Proxy) to convey the original request scheme and host to backend servers (e.g., X-Forwarded-Proto: https and X-Forwarded-Host). The blueprint mandates these headers not be removed or overwritten by inspection or security profiles so backends can correctly interpret original HTTPS requests.

title: HEADER REWRITE RULES
description:
Configured rules in the WAF/Reverse Proxy templates that rewrite request/response headers to preserve HTTPS semantics, support authentication flows, adjust host headers, and prevent redirect/loop issues. The blueprint requires documented header rewrite rules and validation after security patches.
Ruleset on the WAF/Reverse Proxy that rewrites or injects headers (e.g., X-Forwarded-Proto, X-Forwarded-Host) and adjusts host headers for backend interpretation. Documented header rewrite rules are mandatory per the blueprint and must be preserved across template and policy changes.

title: LOAD BALANCING
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Perimeter/load layer capability described as part of the WAF that distributes incoming requests to backend application servers. Load balancing behavior must be consistent with session persistence requirements and WAF/Reverse Proxy templates.

title: SESSION PERSISTENCE / SESSION HANDLING
description: Mechanism to maintain session affinity on the reverse proxy/WAF so that multi-request flows (including authentication flows) are routed consistently to the correct backend instance; included as part of the Portal-App-Template behavior.

title: PARTNER ACCESS
description: Partner-facing access portal/service intended to be published through the perimeter WAF/Reverse Proxy using the Portal-App-Template, requiring header rewrite, session handling and HTTPS semantics preservation.

title: INTERNAL WEB SERVICES WITH EXTERNAL INTERFACE
description: Internal services that expose specific interfaces to external users; intended to be published through the perimeter WAF/Reverse Proxy with TLS termination, header preservation, and template-based protections rather than direct exposure.

title: DEFAULT-STRICT PROFILE
description: A generic WAF security profile referenced by the blueprint as prohibited for productive portals. The blueprint recommends against using a generic "Default-Strict" profile and instead requires dedicated, documented templates per application.

title: INTERNAL APPLICATION ZONE
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Logical internal network segment where backend application servers reside. Isolated from direct Internet access, typically implemented as one or more server VLANs. Backends expect preserved HTTPS semantics (via X-Forwarded headers) even when the perimeter terminates TLS and forwards traffic over HTTP.

title: INTERNET
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Public network/external user plane from which clients access published services. External traffic is received by the Perimeter Zone (WAF/Reverse Proxy) which performs TLS termination, inspection, and forwarding to internal backends.

title: CERTIFICATE SERVICE
description: Service and lifecycle processes for TLS certificates used at the perimeter (WAF) and for backend HTTPS where applicable. Responsible for certificate issuance/renewal and ensuring TLS profiles used by the WAF are current; certificate renewal or misconfiguration is a decision-tree item and can cause broken external access if not validated against the blueprint after updates.

title: WAF PROFILES
description: Configured profiles/templates on the WAF (e.g., Portal-App-Template, Default-Strict) that define inspection policies, header handling, TLS settings, HTTP/2 compatibility, and session rules. The blueprint requires dedicated templates per application and validation of these profiles after security/firmware updates.

title: INSPECTION POLICIES
description: Security inspection rule sets applied by the WAF that perform request/response inspection, may alter or block traffic, and must be checked after updates to ensure custom rules and headers (like X-Forwarded-Proto) are not removed or reset.

title: CERTIFICATE SERVICES & DEVICE AUTHENTICATION (MICROSOFT NDES / CERTIFICATES / INTUNE / AD CS)
description: Blueprint document (version history: 0.1 created 11.02.2026) describing design, decision tree, best practices and operational guidance for Microsoft NDES-based certificate services and their integration into hybrid IT architectures. Covers purpose, licensing notes, recommended separation of Root CA and Issuing CA, monitoring guidance, common failure modes (expired/invalid certificates, misconfigured templates, disrupted communication paths), and step-by-step troubleshooting/installation questions and remediation actions (IIS components, AD CS post-configuration, service account settings, CA/template selection). Includes decision tree and use cases to guide installation, validation, and escalation.

title: NDES (NETWORK DEVICE ENROLLMENT SERVICE)
description: NDES role within Active Directory Certificate Services that automates issuance of certificates to devices, services, and applications. Frequently integrated with Microsoft Intune and network devices for service-based authentication. Relies on IIS components and a correctly configured NDES service account and Issuing CA/template. Failure modes include inability to start service, missing IIS components, misconfiguration in AD CS post-configuration, or broken communication between NDES and the CA.

title: ACTIVE DIRECTORY CERTIFICATE SERVICES (AD CS)
description: Microsoft certificate authority infrastructure that provides Root and Issuing CA roles, certificate templates, and certificate lifecycle management. AD CS is the backend for NDES; AD CS post-configuration must be completed for NDES to operate. Issues in AD CS (misconfigured templates, wrong CA selection, expired CA certificates) manifest as authentication failures, connection drops, or inability to issue certificates.

title: MICROSOFT INTUNE
description: MICROSOFT INTUNE is a cloud-based device management platform that can be optionally integrated with an NDES (Network Device Enrollment Service) connector to provision certificates to managed devices. In hybrid deployments it is a common target for NDES‑supplied certificates: the NDES connector must communicate with the Intune service to deliver SCEP (Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol) certificate profiles and to complete device enrollment. For proper Intune integration, the NDES connector’s healthy state in Azure is required.

title: ROOT CA
description: The offline/trust anchor certificate authority in a PKI hierarchy. Best-practice separation from Issuing CA is recommended by the blueprint. Root CA signs issuing CA certificates and defines trust; problems at Root CA level (expiry, mismanagement) can break certificate validation across the environment.

title: ISSUING CA
description: Operational certificate authority that issues end-entity certificates based on configured certificate templates. NDES must be configured to use the correct Issuing CA and appropriate certificate template for successful certificate issuance.

title: CERTIFICATE TEMPLATES
description: Template definitions used by AD CS/Issuing CA to define certificate properties (key usage, validity, subject fields). Misconfigured templates are a common cause of failures in automated issuance and device authentication workflows and are explicitly called out in the blueprint as an item for documentation and regular review.

title: NDES SERVICE ACCOUNT
description: Service account used by the NDES service (must be assigned and configured properly). The blueprint mandates membership/permissions (example: membership in IIS_IUSRS) and other correct privilege assignments; an incorrectly configured service account prevents NDES from functioning correctly.

title: IIS COMPONENTS (ASP.NET 3.5, ASP.NET 4.7.2, REQUEST FILTERING, IIS 6 MANAGEMENT COMPATIBILITY)
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Required IIS server components and features on the web/application host for NDES to operate. The decision tree explicitly checks for presence of these components; missing components will prevent NDES from starting or functioning and require installation and testing.

title: IIS_IUSRS GROUP
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Built-in IIS user group on Windows/IIS hosts. The NDES service account is expected to be a member of IIS_IUSRS (or otherwise have appropriate permissions) per the blueprint; membership affects service runtime permissions for NDES.

title: AD CS POST-CONFIGURATION FOR NDES
description: Post-installation configuration steps within AD CS necessary for NDES operation (examples: registration of NDES components, enabling required templates, selecting the correct Issuing CA). The decision tree requires this post-configuration to be completed before proceeding to certificate issuance tests.

title: TARGET DEVICES (NETWORK DEVICES / MANAGED ENDPOINTS)
description: Devices and services that request and consume certificates issued via NDES (network devices, Intune-managed endpoints, service appliances). Certificates uniquely identify devices and enable secure communication and automated authentication without user interaction.

title: MONITORING OF CERTIFICATE REQUESTS AND ERRORS
description: Operational monitoring practices and systems recommended in the blueprint to track certificate requests, issuance errors, expiry, and communication failures between NDES, AD CS, and target systems. Monitoring is recommended to detect indirect errors (connection drops, authentication failures, service outages) caused by certificate problems.

title: DECISION TREE – NDES INSTALLATION & OPERATION
description: Structured troubleshooting and installation flow embedded in the blueprint (questions 1–7) that guides technicians through verifying NDES installation/reachability, NDES service start issues, IIS component presence, AD CS post-configuration, service account configuration, CA/template selection, and final certificate issuance testing and escalation steps.

title: IIS HOST / WEB SERVER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: The web/application server instance hosting IIS and the NDES web endpoints; must have required IIS features/components installed (ASP.NET 3.5/4.7.2, Request Filtering, IIS6 Management Compatibility) and use a correctly permissioned NDES service account (membership in IIS_IUSRS) to operate.

title: INFRASTRUCTURE SPECIALISTS
description: Operational/infrastructure specialists (including AD CS specialists) identified as the escalation target when NDES cannot issue certificates after initial troubleshooting; responsible for deeper PKI, CA, and server/infrastructure remediation beyond the NDES decision-tree steps.

title: NETWORK DEVICES
description: Network appliances and devices (routers, switches, firewalls, VPN gateways, etc.) that request and consume certificates issued via NDES for authentication and secure communication; explicitly referenced as common NDES targets in the blueprint.

title: MANAGED ENDPOINTS
description: Client endpoints managed by Intune or similar device management platforms that receive certificates through NDES for device authentication; included as targets for enrollment and issuance verification in the decision tree.

title: KF52: MICROSOFT TEAMS – NOTIFICATIONS NOT DISPLAYED (TI-1615096)
description: Support incident opened 08.10.25 via Portal: Ticket TI-1615096 (KF52: Microsoft Teams – Notifications not displayed). Reported that a user on a laptop stopped receiving Teams pop-up notifications (chats/mentions) though messages were delivered and only visible when Teams was opened. Created 08.10.25 08:41, assigned to Employee 35, investigated and closed 08.10.25 10:22. Closure notes: Notifications restored after enabling Teams notification channel and disabling/adjusting Windows Focus Assist; remediation performed according to the referenced Blueprint.

title: CONTACT PERSON 11
description: CONTACT PERSON 11 is the named customer contact at Customer Company 52 AG who reported the printing-issue impact via the support portal and is listed in the ticket header for TI-931293. They explicitly requested a rollback of the Windows version on the print servers, were the scheduled person to test printing after the restore, and confirmed both the symptom details and the successful resolution.

title: BLUEPRINT – TEAMS NOTIFICATIONS STANDARD – VERSION 1.1
description: Standard troubleshooting and remediation document referenced in the ticket ("Blueprint – Teams Notifications Standard – Version 1.1"). Contains the checklist and steps used during diagnostics (Teams notification settings, Windows Notification Center, Focus Assist, Teams restart) and was followed to resolve the issue.

title: TEAMS NOTIFICATION CHANNEL (BANNER)
description: Application-specific notification control within Microsoft Teams (banner/pop-up notifications). In this incident the Teams notification channel (banner) was disabled in the user's Teams settings, preventing desktop pop-up notifications.

title: WINDOWS FOCUS ASSIST
description: Windows OS feature that suppresses or blocks toast/pop-up notifications (Focus Assist). Focus Assist was active on the user's laptop and was blocking Teams pop-ups; disabling or adjusting it restored notifications.

title: WINDOWS NOTIFICATION CENTER
description: Windows component managing toast notifications and app notification permissions; checks against Notification Center were part of the Blueprint verification to ensure OS-level notification delivery was enabled.

title: LAPTOP
description: The user's endpoint device where the issue occurred (laptop). The problem manifested only on the laptop (not on smartphone) and a recent Windows Update had run the previous week, which was noted during triage.

title: SMARTPHONE
description: A mobile endpoint class referenced in triage questions (helpdesk asked whether the issue also occurred on the smartphone). The smartphone represents an alternate device/platform used to determine whether the problem is device-specific or OS-specific.

title: WINDOWS (OS)
description: The operating system environment on the user's laptop (Windows). The OS provides notification infrastructure (Notification Center), includes Focus Assist as a feature that can suppress pop-ups, and had a recent Windows Update run the prior week which was considered during triage.

title: WINDOWS UPDATE
description: WINDOWS UPDATE — A recent operating-system update that was applied to the print-server virtual machines prior to a VM restore and was noted in the incident ticket as "Windows Update ran last week." The update was identified as part of the root-cause sequence (Windows update → VM restore → network share issues affecting print queues) and was also considered during triage as a potential trigger for changed OS behavior — specifically altered settings or notification behavior that could explain modified Teams pop-ups. In short, the Windows update is a recent, proximate event tied to both the print-queue/network-share disruption after the VM restore and to suspected changes in Teams notification behavior.

title: TI-1569482
description: Support incident opened 18.02.25 (Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Access to project folder denied). Created via Email, assigned to Employee 18, status progressed through remote analysis, Entra ID review, test measures, adjustments and monitoring, and closed 25.02.25. Closure note: Root cause identified (group nesting in Entra ID). Permission structure cleaned up. Access restored. Activity log includes Remote analysis, Entra ID review, test measures, adjustments, monitoring and customer confirmation.

title: CONTACT PERSON 03
description: Named customer contact who reported the access denial to the SharePoint folder /Projects/2025_Migration and provided initial details to the Helpdesk.

title: EMPLOYEE 18
description: EMPLOYEE 18 is a support engineer assigned to ticket TI-1569482 who conducted remote analysis and remediation of Entra ID group membership issues. They reviewed group membership and nesting, applied a direct group assignment as a test measure, corrected group nesting, monitored the outcome, and closed the ticket after confirming access was restored. In parallel, EMPLOYEE 18 performed customer communications and case management: coordinating calls with Contact Person 11, following up with Contact Person 12 to confirm resolution, recording follow‑up entries, providing status updates and escalations within the team, tracking assignment changes, and facilitating transfer of the case to other engineers when required.

title: SHAREPOINT ONLINE
description: SHAREPOINT ONLINE: Microsoft SharePoint Online is the target application/service governed by the referenced document; the document's architecture defines how folder- and library-level permissions are assigned and enforced within SharePoint Online. The service hosts the project site and its folders (for example /Projects/2025_Migration), and the affected folder resides in this environment. SharePoint's permission view was used to inspect effective access and group-based permissions for the folder, reflecting the document's model for permission assignment and enforcement.

title: PROJECT FOLDER /PROJECTS/2025_MIGRATION
description: PROJECT FOLDER /PROJECTS/2025_MIGRATION is a specific SharePoint folder used as a concrete example to illustrate group-based permission assignment. Permissions on this folder were granted to a SharePoint resource group (SP_Project_IT) rather than to individual users. When users attempted to access /PROJECTS/2025_MIGRATION they received the error "Access denied – You do not have the required permissions." Only this folder was affected; other project folders remained accessible. This behavior indicates the folder’s effective access relies on membership and correct application of the SP_Project_IT group (for example, users not being in the group, a provisioning/synchronization issue, or broken permission inheritance could explain the denied access).

title: ENTRA ID
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: ENTRA ID (Azure Active Directory) is the tenant/service used as the authoritative identity directory to manage users, groups and authorizations for Microsoft services. SharePoint evaluates effective folder permissions against current Entra ID group membership and nesting (groups containing other groups), so group nesting in Entra ID determines a user’s effective access. An incorrect group nesting introduced when a new group was created caused a SharePoint permission outage, demonstrating that permission evaluation is driven by the live group-nesting state in Entra ID.

title: IT-PROJEKTE
description: Azure AD security group representing the team "IT-Projekte" (users who should have access to the project folder). This group was expected to provide access via nesting but was incorrectly nested into the old/deactivated group instead of the new permission group.

title: SP_PROJECT_IT
description: New Azure AD security group created to grant access to the SharePoint project folder (listed in SharePoint permission view as granting access). It was created the week before the incident; effective access required IT-Projekte to be nested into this group.

title: SP_PROJECT_IT_V1
description: Old/deactivated Azure AD security group previously used for project permissions ("SP_Project_IT_v1"). IT-Projekte remained nested in this old group after the new group was created, causing users not to receive effective permissions on the new folder until nesting was corrected. The old group was deactivated and later removed.

title: WORKING USER (COMPARISON USER)
description: An individual user used as the comparison case during troubleshooting. This working user was a member of SP_Project_IT_Lead and did not experience the access denial, used to compare membership differences versus affected users.

title: TEST USER (DIRECT ASSIGNMENT)
description: A user account used during the test measures: this user was added directly to the SP_Project_IT group by the support engineer as a test, immediately gained access to the /Projects/2025_Migration folder while the direct assignment was present, and lost access again after removal of the direct assignment—evidence used to confirm the nesting/root cause.

title: MICROSOFT TEAMS FEDERATION & EXTERNAL ACCESS – CUSTOMER COMPANY 52
description: Architecture and configuration blueprint (Version 1.0, Date 25.08.2025) authored by Unified Communications / M365 Engineering. Defines objectives, scope and core principles for secure, controlled Microsoft Teams federation, external access and presence sharing for Customer Company 52 AG (KF52). Contains architecture overview (internal user → Teams Cloud Service → Federation Layer → External tenant), supported communication methods (SIP federation, presence exchange, tenant policy evaluation), federation configuration model, presence sharing parameters and modes, policy dependencies and a decision tree for troubleshooting and validation. Emphasizes that security/external-access changes must be validated against this blueprint and security policies KF52.

title: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / M365 ENGINEERING
description: The authoring team (Unified Communications / M365 Engineering) responsible for creating and maintaining the Teams federation and external access blueprint and for specifying architecture, configuration and security constraints for KF52.

title: TENANT KF52
description: The internal tenant/namespace for Customer Company 52 AG (referred to as KF52) representing the internal user population and tenant-specific configuration context to which the blueprint applies.

title: EXTERNAL TENANT
description: Any external Microsoft 365/Teams tenant with which KF52 may federate under the blueprint rules; partner domains and external tenants are subject to allowed-domain controls when domain restrictions are enabled.

title: TEAMS CLOUD SERVICE
description: Microsoft-hosted Teams service that acts as the central cloud component for chat, presence, call signaling and federation. In the architecture it mediates between internal users (Tenant KF52) and the Federation Layer/external tenants and evaluates tenant policies.

title: FEDERATION LAYER
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Logical/functional layer responsible for inter-tenant federation activities: SIP federation signaling, presence exchange, and enforcement/evaluation of tenant policies for cross-tenant communication. Sits between Teams Cloud Service and external tenants in the blueprint's architecture.

title: SIP FEDERATION
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: The SIP-based signaling mechanism used for federation of calls and presence between tenants. Identified as a primary communication mechanism in the architecture (used by the Federation Layer).

title: FEDERATION CONFIGURATION
description: Set of tenant-level federation controls and configuration options that govern external communications: includes AllowFederatedUsers, AllowPublicUsers and AllowedDomains when domain restrictions are enabled. Determines which external users/domains are permitted to federate and how federation behavior is enforced.

title: ALLOWFEDERATEDUSERS
description: Federation configuration parameter that controls whether users in external federated tenants are allowed to communicate with KF52 users. Part of the federation configuration policy set.

title: ALLOWPUBLICUSERS
description: Federation configuration parameter that controls whether public (non-organizational) users are allowed to interact with KF52 (enables or restricts public user access).

title: ALLOWEDDOMAINS
description: Federation parameter used when domain restrictions are enabled; partner domains must be explicitly listed here to allow federation with those specific external tenants/domains.

title: PRESENCE SHARING
description: Feature and policy area that governs how presence information about internal users is shared with external users. Configurable via parameters such as EnablePresencePrivacyMode and PresenceSharingSetting and can operate in Full Presence, Limited Presence or Disabled modes. Changes to presence sharing affect presence display, signaling and availability synchronization.

title: ENABLEPRESENCEPRIVACYMODE
description: ENABLEPRESENCEPRIVACYMODE (reported as the tenant-level Teams setting "EnablePresencePrivacyMode") is a presence-related configuration flag that, when enabled, activates a privacy mode restricting or altering how user presence is shared with external or federated users. It was observed during Teams PowerShell analysis and is directly relevant to the symptom where external users appear "Offline" even though chat and other communications still function. Because it changes external presence visibility at the tenant level, administrators should consider this setting when troubleshooting cross-tenant/federated presence issues and when evaluating presence/privacy policy impacts.

title: PRESENCESHARINGSETTING
description: Presence-sharing configuration parameter controlling the granularity/mode (Full, Limited, Disabled) of presence information released to external tenants or public users.

title: FULL PRESENCE / LIMITED PRESENCE / DISABLED
description: The operational modes for presence sharing. Full Presence releases normal presence, Limited or Privacy mode may show external users as Offline while still allowing chat, and Disabled prevents presence sharing entirely. Mode selection directly impacts presence display, call signaling and availability synchronization.

title: TEAMS MESSAGING POLICIES
description: Tenant-level Teams messaging policy set referenced as a dependent configuration that can indirectly affect federation and external chat behaviors; modifications may require blueprint validation.

title: EXTERNAL ACCESS POLICIES
description: Policies specifically governing external access behavior (domain allow/block lists, guest/external access controls) that interact with federation configuration and must be validated against the blueprint before changes.

title: CONDITIONAL ACCESS POLICIES
description: Higher-level access control policies (e.g., Conditional Access) that may influence federation, presence sharing and client behavior; listed among policy dependencies to be evaluated when diagnosing federation/external-access issues.

title: PRIVACY MODE SETTINGS
description: Settings that determine privacy behavior for presence (e.g., whether privacy mode is enabled) and are explicitly called out as having potential impact on external presence display and signaling.

title: TENANT FEDERATION CONFIGURATION
description: The tenant-specific federation settings and state (a specific instance of federation configuration for a tenant) that the blueprint references as a dependency and validation point for changes to external access or security policies.

title: CHAT
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Real-time text messaging traffic and capabilities exchanged across tenants via the Federation Layer and Teams Cloud Service; chat delivery is a primary service function that federation and external access policies must preserve.

title: CALL SIGNALING
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Signaling flows (SIP or similar) used to set up, modify and terminate calls across tenants; handled through the Federation Layer and SIP federation mechanisms and influenced by federation and external access policies.

title: TENANT POLICY EVALUATION
description: The process executed (typically in the Teams Cloud Service/Federation Layer) to evaluate tenant-level policies (e.g., messaging, external access, conditional access) when deciding whether to allow or modify cross-tenant interactions such as chat, presence and call signaling.

title: SECURITY POLICIES KF52
description: Organization-specific security policy set referenced by the blueprint (KF52) that constrains federation/external-access behavior; changes to these security policies must be validated against the blueprint to avoid unintended impact on federation and presence.

title: DOMAIN RESTRICTIONS
description: Configuration mode within federation settings that enforces domain-level restrictions — when enabled only domains explicitly listed in AllowedDomains are permitted to federate; impacts partner onboarding and cross-tenant connectivity.

title: PARTNER DOMAINS
description: External domain names (partner domains) representing external tenants or organizations that may be permitted for federation; these domain names must be present in AllowedDomains when domain restrictions are enabled and are resolved/validated via DNS/name services.

title: PRESENCE DISPLAY
description: The observable presence state (online/away/offline/limited) shown to external users about KF52 users; an operational outcome of Presence Sharing settings and presence exchange and can be impacted by EnablePresencePrivacyMode or PresenceSharingSetting.

title: TI-1622483
description: Support incident opened 06.11.25 (Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs). Company: Customer Company 60 AG. Contact: Contact Person 15. Owner: Employee 21. Origin: Email. Created: 06.11.25 08:37. Closed: 12.11.25 14:52. Closure note: "Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored." Ticket activity documents remote troubleshooting, network segmentation discovery, coordination with Network Team, tests, and final customer confirmation.

title: CONTACT PERSON 15
description: Named customer contact who reported that the printer MFP-Basel-01 was not responding to print jobs and provided follow-up confirmation when the printer was restored.

title: PRINT QUEUE
description: The server-side queue where submitted print jobs were held; jobs were reported to remain in the queue and in some cases disappear without being printed, indicating delivery/communication problems between server and printer.

title: NETWORK SEGMENT FOR DEVICES
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: The new network segment introduced for devices the week prior to the incident — implemented as a separate VLAN for printers/devices at the Basel site; part of the recent infrastructure change that caused segmentation.

title: FIREWALL RULE
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Access control rule on the site's firewall that, as initially configured, did not allow traffic from the server network to the printer VLAN. The rule was adjusted by the Network Team to permit required routing/traffic and restore printing functionality.

title: USER WORKSTATIONS
description: End-user devices from which print jobs were submitted; user workstation tests were performed during verification after the fix to ensure printing succeeded for users as well as the server.

title: PRINT & SCANNING INFRASTRUCTURE STANDARD - VERSION 1.0
description: Infrastructure standard/document referenced during troubleshooting. Contains Location Concept and requirements that printers may operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server; guidance used to validate expected configuration and to implement the corrective change.

title: LOCATION NETWORK (BASEL)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
