description: The local user/network segment at the Basel site where client workstations reside. A client on this location network could successfully ping the printer, indicating client-to-printer reachability within the location network despite server-to-printer segmentation.

title: ROUTING
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Routing between network segments/VLANs required to allow traffic (e.g., print job delivery) from the Print Server's network to the Printer VLAN. The Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard requires routing be configured so the print server can reach printers.

title: BU UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS
description: Specialist business unit/team responsible for Teams Direct Routing, media flow and PSTN integration; ticket was forwarded here for deeper VoIP/Direct Routing analysis.

title: PSTN
description: Public switched telephone network connectivity to which Teams Direct Routing connects via the SBC; external calls (incoming/outgoing PSTN) were affected, while internal Teams calls were not.

title: RTP STREAM
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Real-time Transport Protocol media streams conveying voice. Analysis showed RTP from Microsoft IP was visible but RTP from internal clients was not consistently visible to SBC, resulting in one-way audio. RTP port ranges and bidirectional media flow were key elements.

title: SIP SIGNALING
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Session Initiation Protocol signaling tracked in SBC logs and Teams Admin Center; SIP call setup (INVITE/200 OK) succeeded without errors, indicating signaling was not the failing component.

title: FIREWALL RULE – OUTBOUND PORT RESTRICTION 3478–3481 ONLY
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Specific firewall rule implemented at the Zurich location that restricted outbound UDP traffic to only the defined port range 3478–3481. This rule allowed STUN/TURN/other signalling ranges but blocked the dynamic RTP port range used for voice, preventing client RTP from reaching the SBC.

title: TEAMS MEDIA PORTS (UDP 3478–3481; RTP 49152–53247)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Port requirements documented for Teams media: UDP 3478–3481 (STUN/TURN/ICE) and dynamic RTP ports 49152–53247. The firewall rule only allowed 3478–3481 and blocked the dynamic RTP range, which caused media path failure when Media Bypass was in use.

title: FIREWALL LOGS
description: Location firewall logs reviewed during troubleshooting; they revealed the new outbound restriction rule and supported the correlation between firewall change and onset of audio issues.

title: MEDIA BYPASS
type: UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS / DIRECT ROUTING (SBC, MEDIA FLOW)
description: Direct Routing/media-flow mode in which client media (RTP) is sent directly from client to SBC (bypassing media relay), making client-side outbound port availability critical. Zurich site used Media Bypass, which required the dynamic RTP ports to be open outbound.

title: HEADSETS
description: Various headset models used by affected users; tests showed multiple headsets were affected, ruling out a single-device hardware fault.

title: INTERNET / PACKET LOSS CHECKS
description: Network checks performed during troubleshooting that showed internet was stable and no packet loss was visible, helping to focus investigation on firewall port restrictions rather than general connectivity issues.

title: TEMPORARY UDP PORT OPENING (TEST)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: A temporary change during testing that opened a wider UDP port range (including dynamic RTP ports). Result: audio worked immediately, confirming firewall port restriction as root cause. Reapplying restriction removed audio again.

title: MONITORING (48H)
description: Post-remediation monitoring period of 48 hours without anomalies was used to validate stability of the fix before closing the ticket.

title: NAT (NAT TRAVERSAL)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Network Address Translation and NAT traversal behaviors referenced in the Blueprint and analysis — relevant to media path establishment, port mappings, and whether Media Bypass will allow direct client-to-SBC RTP flows. Impacts how client RTP reaches the SBC across the site edge.

title: STUN/TURN/ICE
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Protocols/mechanisms used to establish and traverse media paths for Teams (STUN/TURN/ICE). Ports UDP 3478–3481 are associated with these services; the firewall rule allowed only these ports while blocking the dynamic RTP port range required for direct media, contributing to the outage.

title: SECURITY HARDENING CHANGE
description: A location-specific security-hardening change applied over the weekend at the Zurich site that resulted in modification of firewall outbound rules — the operational change that introduced the restrictive outbound port policy implicated as the root cause.

title: SDP (MEDIA NEGOTIATION)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Session Description Protocol (SDP) media-negotiation step observed in SBC logs ("Media negotiation (SDP) successful") — part of call setup indicating media codecs/ports were negotiated correctly even though RTP was not consistently flowing from the client side.

title: SHAREPOINT PERMISSION & GROUP ARCHITECTURE – CUSTOMER COMPANY 25
description: Document Version 1.0 (Date: 20.06.2025) authored by M365 / Identity Engineering describing architectural and governance specifications for permission structures in SharePoint Online at Customer Company 25 AG. Purpose: define a consistent, maintainable, auditable permission model for project folders in SharePoint document libraries. Core rules include no direct user permissions on folders/libraries, permissions assigned exclusively via dedicated Azure AD groups, separation of functional groups vs. SharePoint resource groups, documented group nesting, and documented group lifecycle and synchronization requirements. Includes decision tree, examples, and procedural requirements for unique permissions and group changes.

title: M365 / IDENTITY ENGINEERING
description: The authoring team responsible for creating the document; an internal Microsoft 365 / Identity-focused engineering function that defined the group and permission model, lifecycle rules, and operational guidance.

title: FUNCTIONAL GROUP – IT-PROJECTS
description: Example functional group representing an organizational/team membership ("IT-Projects"). Functional groups are the source of users (members) and must not receive SharePoint permissions directly; they are intended to be nested into SharePoint resource groups.

title: FUNCTIONAL GROUP – FINANCE-TEAM
description: Example functional group representing Finance; used to illustrate separation between functional groups and resource groups and that functional groups should not be granted permissions directly in SharePoint.

title: FUNCTIONAL GROUP – RISK-TEAM
description: Example functional group representing Risk; same role as other functional groups in the model.

title: SHAREPOINT RESOURCE GROUP – SP_PROJECT_IT
description: Example SharePoint resource (Azure AD) group used to hold permissions on SharePoint folders/libraries (e.g., SP_Project_IT grants access to /Projects/2025_Migration). Functional groups are nested into these resource groups to provide effective permissions.

title: SHAREPOINT RESOURCE GROUP – SP_PROJECT_IT_LEAD
description: Example resource group intended for project leads; in the document's example it is shown nested into or connected with SP_Project_IT to illustrate group nesting relationships.

title: USER
description: Generic end user/member referenced by the architecture as the actor who gains effective access indirectly: a User is member of a functional group, which is nested into a SharePoint resource group that holds folder/library permissions.

title: GROUP NESTING
description: Conceptual/configuration construct describing the hierarchical membership of functional groups within SharePoint resource groups in Entra ID/Azure AD. Document emphasizes correct nesting is required because SharePoint evaluates effective permissions from current nesting and incorrect nesting leads to missing permissions.

title: GROUP LIFECYCLE & SYNCHRONIZATION
description: Operational process and controls covering naming uniqueness, removal of historical groups, documentation of group changes, and waiting/verification for synchronization/propagation after group modifications to ensure effective permissions update correctly in SharePoint.

title: PERMISSION MODEL
description: The document's formal policy for assigning permissions: folder/library permissions must be assigned only to SharePoint resource groups (not to users directly), with rules for unique permissions, verification steps, and examples of permission assignments.

title: UNIQUE PERMISSIONS
description: Operational rule and control governing when folder/library-level unique permissions may be created: requires documentation of referenced resource group, verification that no old/deactivated group is still nested, and confirmation that target group is active.

title: ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW
description: Design subsection that diagrammatically and textually describes how Users -> Functional Group membership -> Functional Group nested into SharePoint Resource Group -> Resource Group has folder permission results in effective access; includes the permission propagation model and design principles.

title: NO DIRECT USER PERMISSIONS
description: Policy that forbids assigning permissions directly to individual user accounts at folder or library level in SharePoint; enforces that access must be granted via group memberships only.

title: GROUP-BASED PERMISSIONS (DEDICATED AZURE AD GROUPS)
description: Policy mandate that all SharePoint permissions are granted exclusively via dedicated Azure AD (Entra ID) groups (SharePoint resource groups) and not via functional groups or direct user assignments; includes naming and lifecycle constraints for those groups.

title: DOCUMENTED GROUP NESTING
description: Requirement that all group nesting relationships (which functional groups are nested into which resource groups) must be documented and kept current in Entra ID to ensure correct effective permissions in SharePoint; incorrect or undocumented nesting leads to missing access.

title: NO PARALLEL OR HISTORICAL GROUPS
description: Rule prohibiting the simultaneous existence of old/deprecated resource groups alongside new ones for the same purpose (e.g., *_v1); requires removal of historical groups before deactivation of new ones to prevent conflicting nesting and permission ambiguity.

title: GROUP NAMING & DEACTIVATION RULES
description: Operational rules specifying unique naming for new project/resource groups, procedures for deactivating historical groups, verifying references to old versions, and documenting group changes to prevent duplicate scope and ensure clean lifecycle transitions.

title: SYNCHRONIZATION & PROPAGATION (SYNC TIME)
description: Operational note that after group changes administrators must wait for directory synchronization/propagation, then verify effective permissions in SharePoint; implies monitoring/verifying propagation and checking effective permission outcomes as part of change processes.

title: MANAGEMENT REPORTING & FINANCIAL REPORTING INFRASTRUCTURE STANDARD V1.0
description: Approved architecture and operational standard (dated 12.04.2024, Status: Approved) that specifies the design, security configuration and operational procedures for the Financial Reporting (Management Reporter / Financial Reports) infrastructure within the D365 Finance & Operations environment of Customer Company 11. Objectives include stable Report Designer startup, access to existing reports, consistent report metadata, high-performance report generation and TLS-compliant security configuration. The document lists dependencies (AOS servers, Financial Reporting Data Mart, IIS components, TLS/Cipher Suite configuration, service accounts, SQL backend), environment consistency requirements (UAT, PROD), operational principles (synchronous cipher-suite changes, AOS restarts after adjustments, Data Mart reset in case of data inconsistency), a decision tree for troubleshooting and use cases, and concrete checks for issues (Data Mart reset, SQL connection, AOS service, IIS configuration, cipher-suite/TLS changes, Windows updates, service account authentication and TLS compatibility).

title: FINANCIAL REPORTING SERVICE
description: Core reporting application/service that hosts the Report Designer and the Report Rendering Engine. It provides the runtime to design and render financial reports and interacts with the AOS server and the Data Mart for data and metadata. It is a primary component of the reporting architecture.

title: FINANCIAL REPORTING DATA MART
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Aggregated SQL-based data store that provides financial reporting data to the Financial Reporting Service and Report Designer. The Data Mart must be consistent and may require a reset in case of data inconsistency. It is a critical dependency for report availability and correctness.

title: IIS / TLS LAYER
description: IIS-based layer responsible for TLS termination and Cipher Suite configuration for the reporting infrastructure. This layer controls TLS connections and allowed cipher suites according to company standards and Microsoft recommendations; misconfiguration here can block or degrade report access and functionality.

title: TLS / CIPHER SUITE CONFIGURATION
description: The TLS and allowed Cipher Suite settings applied to the IIS / TLS layer (and more broadly across environments). The standard mandates TLS-compliant configuration and uniform cipher-suite settings across UAT and PROD. Misconfigured cipher suites or TLS policies can cause slow Report Designer startups, authentication failures, unknown report authors, and reports not loading. Changes must be synchronized across environments.

title: SERVICE ACCOUNTS
description: Service accounts used by reporting components to access data and metadata (e.g., for Data Mart access, report processing and author attribution). The document highlights service account connectivity as a troubleshooting check when report authors appear as “unknown” and emphasizes consistent configuration of these accounts across environments.

title: SQL BACKEND
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Relational database backend that hosts the Data Mart and other reporting metadata; SQL connectivity is a primary troubleshooting item (check SQL connection) and the SQL backend is a dependency for report rendering and Data Mart operations.

title: USER (BROWSER / REPORT DESIGNER)
description: End users or client endpoints interacting with the reporting system either via browser-based interfaces or the Report Designer client. The user is the origin of report requests and is affected by TLS, AOS, IIS and Data Mart availability and performance.

title: REPORT METADATA
description: Metadata describing reports (authors, timestamps, definitions, locations) used by the Report Designer and Financial Reporting Service. Stored/retrieved via the SQL backend/Data Mart and used for author attribution and locating reports; metadata visibility and correctness are affected by service account authentication and TLS compatibility.

title: CIPHER SUITES
description: The specific TLS cipher suites permitted by company policy and implemented on the IIS / TLS layer. Changes to allowed cipher suites are managed as part of configuration management and must be synchronized across environments; incompatible cipher suites can break TLS negotiation between clients, AOS, IIS and SQL, degrading or blocking report operations.

title: TLS POLICIES
description: Higher-level TLS configuration and policies (protocol versions, minimum TLS level, cipher suite selection) applied to the reporting infrastructure (IIS / TLS layer, AOS, database connections). TLS policy changes can require coordinated updates and service restarts and are listed as primary diagnostic items in the decision tree.

title: WINDOWS SECURITY UPDATE
description: Operating system updates (security patches) on servers or clients that can alter TLS behavior, available cipher suites, or cryptographic libraries. The decision tree includes checking whether a Windows security update was installed as such updates can change TLS compatibility and cause cross-environment issues.

title: DATA MART RESET
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Operational action/maintenance on the Financial Reporting Data Mart that clears and rebuilds aggregated reporting data to fix data inconsistency. The document specifies Data Mart reset as a remedial step when issues are isolated to a single environment and data inconsistency is suspected.

title: NETWORK LATENCY
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Network performance characteristic (latency) that can impact Report Designer startup times and rendering performance. The decision tree lists checking network latency when Report Designer is extremely slow, and it may indicate network or infrastructure issues outside the application stack.

title: UNKNOWN REPORT AUTHOR SYMPTOM
description: Operational symptom where reports show authors as “unknown”; the document maps this symptom to checks such as service account connectivity, authentication handshake and TLS compatibility. It represents an observable metadata/logging issue tied to authentication and metadata retrieval.

title: TI-1607314
description: Support incident opened 12.08.25 (Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External users permanently displayed as “Offline”). Created via email, assigned to Employee 37, investigated and closed 22.08.25. Closure: "Analysis completed. Configuration adjusted according to Teams Federation Blueprint. Presence display correct again." Activities include customer reports, helpdesk inquiries, internal analysis, Teams Admin Center and PowerShell checks, tests, configuration adjustment, and coordination with the customer.

title: EMPLOYEE 37
description: Support engineer/owner assigned to TI-1607314 who coordinated investigation, performed or oversaw analysis (Teams Admin Center, PowerShell), testing, and applied configuration adjustments according to the Teams Federation Blueprint before closing the ticket.

title: TECHBUILD AG
description: External partner company identified by the customer as primarily affected (presence shown as "Offline"); noted to have recently migrated to Direct Routing, which was relevant to the diagnosis.

title: TECHBUILD.CH
description: SIP domain of TechBuild AG referenced in the investigation; the ticket asked whether the SIP domain had changed (customer confirmed domain remained techbuild.ch).

title: BLUEPRINT – MICROSOFT TEAMS FEDERATION & EXTERNAL ACCESS – VERSION 1.4
description: Internal architected guidance referenced during analysis. Contains sections on Federation vs Presence Sharing, impact of Privacy Mode, behavior under restrictive policies, and required settings for partners using Direct Routing. The ticket resolution followed this blueprint.

title: EXTERNAL ACCESS POLICY
description: Teams Admin Center configuration/policy controlling external access and presence-sharing behaviors. The ticket notes "External Access Policy" settings were reviewed (domain allowed but "Presence sharing" was set to "Limited" in the new policy) and a policy change contributed to the issue.

title: GET-CS TENANTFEDERATIONCONFIGURATION
description: Teams PowerShell cmdlet used to retrieve the tenant federation configuration. It returned configuration values (AllowPublicUsers, AllowFederatedUsers, EnablePresencePrivacyMode) that were central to diagnosing presence/federation behavior.

title: PRESENCE SHARING (SETTING)
description: A specific setting within the External Access Policy that controls how presence information is shared with external/federated domains. In the incident it was observed as set to "Limited", which correlated with external users appearing permanently "Offline".

title: TEAMS SECURITY POLICIES
description: Broader set of Teams-related security policies configured by the customer; the customer reported they had recently made external communication more restrictive, which affected presence behavior and required review against the federation blueprint.

title: SECURITY POLICY (RESTRICT EXTERNAL PRESENCE)
description: The new/recently introduced customer policy that restricted external presence information. This policy change conflicted with the tenant's federation configuration/standards and was identified as the root cause requiring remediation.

title: TEST TENANT
description: TEST TENANT is an isolated, non‑production tenant/environment used to reproduce and validate observed issues before applying changes to production. In the TEST TENANT engineers created a folder with unique permissions, then applied a Sensitivity Label afterward, and exercised different user accounts to reproduce token caching/renewal behavior that produced temporary access followed by denial when tokens renewed. The TEST TENANT was also used to temporarily adjust presence settings and validate that presence could be displayed correctly; those temporary changes confirmed the impact of the configuration change and were reverted prior to returning to production.

title: PRODUCTION CONFIGURATION
description: The live/production presence and federation configuration. Reverting from test adjustments back to the production configuration reproduced the "Offline" symptom, indicating the production configuration contained the problematic setting/policy.

title: INTERNAL LOGS
description: Internal logging data and analyses reviewed during the investigation to observe presence, chat delivery, and call signaling behavior and to correlate configuration changes with observed symptoms.

title: KF60: PRINT JOB REMAINS STUCK IN QUEUE (TI-1621044)
description: Support incident opened 21.10.25 for Customer Company 60 AG reporting documents not printing (ticket created via phone at 09:11). Contact: Contact Person 04. Assigned owner: Employee 12. Activities: phone report, remote check, queue activation and restart of print jobs, test print successful. Closed 21.10.25 09:38 with closure note: "Printer queue paused. Queue activated and print jobs restarted."

title: CONTACT PERSON 04
description: Named contact (Ms. Contact Person 04) who reported the incident by phone, described that documents were shown in Windows but not printing, and confirmed resolution after remediation.

title: PRINTER
description: PRINTER: a physical or networked shared printing device at Customer Company 60 AG that served as the target for reported print jobs which affected multiple colleagues. The device is intended to be automatically mapped for users at that location via Group Policy; although the printer was visible and usable by existing colleagues, a newly onboarded employee could not access it until the relevant GPO was applied. After the Group Policy update the PRINTER was automatically mapped for the new user and a test print succeeded.

title: PRINTER QUEUE
description: The print spool/queue associated with the printer that held the print jobs. Queue status was "Paused" (preventing jobs from printing); the queue was activated and jobs restarted to resolve the issue.

title: PRINT JOBS
description: Queued print tasks shown in Windows on the user's workstation; these jobs were present but not processed while the print queue was paused and were restarted by support.

title: WINDOWS WORKSTATION
description: WINDOWS WORKSTATION — The user's PC running Windows hosted the Teams client and a USB headset and provided the OS‑level audio stack; initially the OS audio input showed no signal until the headset microphone cable was reseated, restoring input. The same Windows environment also displayed a print job in its print queue (the OS indicated the job was present) but the job did not progress to printing.

title: CONTACT PERSON 12
description: CONTACT PERSON 12 is the customer-side contact and Head of Customer Services at Customer Company 13 AG. They served as the primary responder in the support ticket thread and the person Employee 17 planned to speak with directly, participating in case management and next‑step coordination. CONTACT PERSON 12 reported that printing was initially still not working, later confirmed that the issue was resolved, and therefore is both an affected stakeholder and the authoritative confirmation source for remediation.

title: TOM
description: Customer-side IT stakeholder (signed emails as "Tom") identified as Head of IT Switzerland Informatics at Customer Company 13; sent appreciative feedback after resolution and used contact email ContactPerson13@kf13.com in correspondence.

title: EMPLOYEE 17
description: EMPLOYEE 17 is a Senior System Engineer in Core Engineering at Consulting Sample Company AG who became the ticket assignee after an internal consultation and was tasked to discuss the issue directly with Contact Person 12. As ticket owner, Employee 17 identified the root cause, performed remediation by disabling and re‑enabling the network share on the print server queues, authored the resolution notes, and closed the ticket.

title: EMPLOYEE 19
description: EMPLOYEE 19 is a Service Manager at Consulting Sample Company who also performed hands‑on engineering work on the incident ticket. They were contacted and asked to take over analysis of blue‑screen (BSOD) and printing issues, conducted further technical troubleshooting using event/log data, and sent an internal thank‑you/praise email recognizing colleagues’ support on the ticket while relaying the customer’s gratitude.

title: EMPLOYEE 20
description: EMPLOYEE 20 is a Consulting Sample Company support engineer who participated in resolving a printing-failures incident. As part of the support team, EMPLOYEE 20 was assigned or volunteered to investigate whether the cause lay on the SAP side (examining SAP-to-printer/print-server interactions) rather than with the print servers themselves. EMPLOYEE 20 was mentioned in internal praise for their role in the resolution and was a recipient of customer praise conveyed via EMPLOYEE 19.

title: EMPLOYEE 26
description: EMPLOYEE 26 is a Consulting Sample Company staff member who serves as Head of Core Engineering and is part of the company’s engineering leadership. The ticket record shows that EMPLOYEE 26 was assigned to the incident on 02.12.24, became the final assignee after internal transfers, and is listed as the ticket owner at closure — indicating they were the internal owner likely responsible for follow-up and formal closure. Internal communications also record EMPLOYEE 26 as having reacted positively to the resolution.

title: CONSULTING SAMPLE COMPANY AG
description: CONSULTING SAMPLE COMPANY AG is the external consulting and service-provider organization that operates the customer helpdesk, appears in helpdesk contact details and closure signatures, and serves as the primary point of contact for incidents and ticket closure. It provides end-to-end ticket lifecycle management — receiving, troubleshooting, coordinating remediation, and closing tickets — and is responsible for incident handling and technical remediation. The organisation includes functional teams and roles such as Core Engineering, Service Management, and CRM, and its named representatives include Employee 17 (Senior System Engineer), Employee 19 (Service Manager), and Employee 23 (CRM), among others, who respectively cover technical engineering, service/SLAs and customer relationship responsibilities.

title: CORE ENGINEERING
description: Internal engineering team within Consulting Sample Company responsible for core systems and infrastructure; Employee 17 (Senior System Engineer) and Employee 26 (Head of Core Engineering) are associated with this team and performed/responsible for remediation actions.

title: PRINT SERVER VRINFRGERPRNT03
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Named print server (VRINFRGERPRNT03) used for SAP printing; specifically identified as having network share problems after a Windows update and VM restore. Administrators disabled and re-enabled network shares on its printer queues to clear pending jobs and restore printing.

title: PRINT SERVER VRINFRGERPRNT04
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Second named print server (VRINFRGERPRNT04) referenced as part of the SAP printing path; printing via VRINFRGERPRNT03 and VRINFRGERPRNT04 was reported as not possible during the incident.

title: VIRTUAL MACHINES (PRINT SERVER VMS)
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: The two virtual machines (VMs) hosting print server services underwent a restore after a Windows update; the VM restore/activity is implicated in causing the print server network share issues that blocked SAP printing.

title: PRINTER NETWORK SHARE
description: The SMB/file-share exposure used to present printer queues from the print servers to clients/SAP. After the Windows update and VM restore, network shares for printer queues on the print servers malfunctioned and had to be disabled and re-enabled to clear stuck jobs and restore printing.

title: PRINTER QUEUES
description: The configured queues on print servers (the targets for SAP print jobs). Pending documents were held on these queues until the network share was re-enabled; disabling/re-enabling queues cleared pending jobs and allowed printing to resume.

title: FOLLOW-ME PRINT SOLUTION
description: The follow-me printing solution referenced as an alternative print path where normal (non-SAP) printing continued to work via other print servers; indicates a separate printing infrastructure that was not affected.

title: SAP
description: Business application (SAP) from which print jobs originate; SAP printing relied on the named print servers (VRINFRGERPRNT03/04) and was blocked during the incident until print server shares/queues were restored.

title: CONTACTPERSON13@KF13.COM
description: Customer-side email address used in the ticket thread (used by "Tom" / Head of IT Switzerland Informatics at Customer Company 13 to send acknowledgements and feedback).

title: WEB TICKETING PORTAL
description: The web-based ticketing/CRM interface used to create and log the ticket and system events (log entries show "WEB" as source for ticket creation and classification actions).

title: CRM
description: Customer Relationship Management team/system referenced in the ticket logs ("Admin, CRM" and "ApplicationUser, CRM Development"); responsible for ticket intake/classification and internal customer-service workflows.

title: PRINT SERVERS (GENERIC)
description: Generic category of print servers used for SAP and normal printing (specific instances include VRINFRGERPRNT03 and VRINFRGERPRNT04; other print servers handled follow-me printing and were not affected).

title: PRINTING INCIDENT - SAP PRINT JOBS FAILED
description: Support incident recorded across 17–19.07.24 (classified PRIO HIGH) because orders could not be printed from SAP, preventing deliveries. Symptoms: printing via two SAP-designated print servers (VRINFRGERPRNT03 / VRINFRGERPRNT04) failed; print spooler restart attempted; infrastructure on‑call activated; restore to state before maintenance window (17.07 at 21:00) performed; subsequent blue screens observed on affected servers; ticket reassigned through multiple engineers (Employees 23, 19, 17) with customer contact persons involved and request to rollback Windows version on the print servers. Activities recorded via WEB entries and assignments by Admin (CRM).

title: OTHER PRINT SERVERS (FOLLOW-ME SOLUTION)
description: Other print servers in the environment providing a follow‑me printing solution; normal printing via these other servers continued to work, indicating the problem was scoped to the VRINFRGERPRNT03/04 servers rather than the entire print infrastructure.

title: PRINT SPOOLER
description: Operating system/service component that manages print job spooling on Windows print servers. The spooler service was restarted by Employee 23 as an initial remediation step; restart did not restore SAP printing functionality on the affected servers.

title: WINDOWS UPDATE / WINDOWS VERSION
description: The Windows OS version or recent Windows updates on the print servers are suspected as the root cause of the printing failure. Contact Person 11 explicitly requested rollback of the Windows version on the print servers; the infrastructure on‑call documented suspicion that a Windows update affected print functionality.

title: INFRASTRUCTURE ON-CALL
description: The on‑call infrastructure operations team that was activated to restore the SAP print servers to their prior state after the incident was classified high priority. This team coordinated/authorized the restore to the pre‑maintenance snapshot (17.07 21:00) and worked with on‑site/other technicians.

title: INTER12203
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Named system identifier mentioned in the restore action entry. Inter12203 is referenced alongside Inter12204 when the restore was performed with Mister X. Likely a maintenance/restore host or related restoration context used during recovery operations (log text does not fully disambiguate whether these are team IDs or host names).

title: INTER12204
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Named system identifier paired with Inter12203 in the restore activity. Referenced as part of the restore operation performed with Mister X; likely a restore/maintenance host or related context used during recovery.

title: MISTER X
description: Technician or engineer referenced by name (anonymized) who participated in the restore action ("Restore performed with Mister X of Inter12203 and Inter12204"). Worked with the infrastructure/on‑call team to perform the restore; role implies hands‑on remediation.

title: BLUE SCREENS (BSOD)
description: System crash symptom observed when printing continued to fail after the restore — reported by Contact Person 11 on 19.07.24 as "blue screens occur." Indicates server instability/crash on the affected print servers after attempted recovery.

title: EARLY SHIFT
description: Operations shift/team that received follow-up information after the restore action ("Information sent to early shift") to continue testing/monitoring the printing situation the next morning.

title: TICKETING PORTAL (WEB)
description: The web-based ticketing/portal system or customer portal where the incident entries were recorded (log lines show "| WEB"), used for recording activity entries, status changes, assignments, and notes related to the printing incident.

title: SAP PRINT SERVERS
description: Logical grouping/category of print & scanning infrastructure that serves SAP print jobs (includes the specific servers VRINFRGERPRNT03 and VRINFRGERPRNT04); responsible for processing SAP-generated print output such as order/delivery documents.

title: TI-1578891
description: Support incident opened 18.03.25 (Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Project folder “Compliance_Review” partially not visible). Reported via Email by Contact Person 02 for Customer Company 25 AG. Assigned to Employee 27, later escalated to BU Infrastructure – M365 / Identity and taken over by System Engineer 11. Issue: inconsistent access to SharePoint folder /Projects/Compliance_Review_2025 for some users; investigation notes, permission checks, Azure AD sign-in log analysis and Microsoft Purview review recorded.

title: CONTACT PERSON 02
description: CONTACT PERSON 02 is the named customer contact at Customer Company 25 AG who initiated the ticket reporting missing folder access. They provided key diagnostic details (affected users, browser information, and the error message), replied to troubleshooting questions, and was addressed in the ticket closure. They served as the primary liaison for the audit/permission issue and was the recipient of results communication, including confirmation that access had been restored.

title: SYSTEM ENGINEER 11
description: Engineer who took over the ticket after escalation (22.03.25) and performed in-depth analysis including Sensitivity Label review, membership tests, and hypothesis of conflict between SharePoint permissions and AIP label encryption.

title: BU INFRASTRUCTURE – M365 / IDENTITY
description: Escalation group responsible for M365 and Identity services within the support organization; assigned the ticket for deeper analysis of SharePoint / Azure AD / Information Protection interactions.

title: /PROJECTS/COMPLIANCE_REVIEW_2025 (COMPLIANCE_REVIEW_2025)
description: Project folder in SharePoint (renamed from "Compliance_Audit" to "Compliance_Review_2025" ~2 weeks earlier). Folder has Unique Permissions and is the object for which some users intermittently see "Item not found". Sensitivity Label "Confidential – Internal" was applied after rename and enforces encryption and access restrictions tied to the Azure AD group "Compliance_Core".

title: M. KELLER
description: One of three named affected users who could not access the Compliance_Review_2025 folder (reported in ticket activities). Member of COMPLIANCE_TEAM per checks.

title: S. BRAUN
description: One of three named affected users who could not access the Compliance_Review_2025 folder (reported in ticket activities). Member of COMPLIANCE_TEAM per checks.

title: T. WIDMER
description: One of three named affected users who could not access the Compliance_Review_2025 folder (reported in ticket activities). Member of COMPLIANCE_TEAM per checks; one affected user works 50% in two teams due to internal department change.

title: COMPLIANCE_TEAM
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Azure AD group created during project group restructuring; reported as included in the SharePoint permission group "SP_Compliance_Main". Tokens/claims observed for affected users contained a claim for COMPLIANCE_TEAM. Membership did not consistently guarantee access to the labeled/encrypted folder in all tests.

title: COMPLIANCE_EXT
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Azure AD group created during the recent restructuring (external/extended compliance group). Mentioned as a newly created group but not directly shown as having explicit access to the folder in the ticket notes.

title: COMPLIANCE_CORE
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Azure AD group required by the Sensitivity Label "Confidential – Internal" as the allowed access group for encrypted content. Label enforcement limits access to members of Compliance_Core; tests showed adding a user directly to Compliance_Core immediately granted access to the folder.

title: SENSITIVITY LABEL – CONFIDENTIAL – INTERNAL
description: Microsoft Information Protection sensitivity label applied to the folder/document library after the rename. Label enforces encryption and restricts access to members of the Azure AD group "Compliance_Core". This label is suspected to conflict with SharePoint's existing unique permissions/group-based access (SP_Compliance_Main) leading to inconsistent access behavior.

title: AZURE AD SIGN-IN LOGS
description: AZURE AD SIGN-IN LOGS were consulted and analyzed during the investigation to inspect issued tokens and claims for the affected users. The logs showed the users' tokens contained a claim for "COMPLIANCE_TEAM" but did not include a claim for "Compliance_Core." Conditional Access evaluation recorded in the sign-in logs was successful and showed no blocking policy or other obvious blocks preventing the sign-ins.

title: MICROSOFT PURVIEW / INFORMATION PROTECTION
description: Information protection system (Microsoft Purview / AIP) used for in-depth review of label application and encryption behavior. Investigated to determine how the Sensitivity Label and AIP encryption interact with SharePoint permissions.

title: AZURE AD TOKEN CLAIMS
description: Authentication token claims observed in Azure AD sign-in logs. Affected users' tokens included a claim for COMPLIANCE_TEAM but did not include a claim for Compliance_Core; token claims are relevant because label-based encryption checks rely on group membership claims to permit access.

title: CONDITIONAL ACCESS EVALUATION
description: Reference to Conditional Access evaluation performed during sign-in: logs indicate Conditional Access evaluation succeeded and did not block access. Noted to rule out Conditional Access as the direct cause of denial; relevant to overall access flow and troubleshooting.

title: EDGE BROWSER
description: Client environment used for accessing SharePoint (Edge browser). Affected users accessed via Edge; tests included Incognito mode and other browsers. Incognito mode showed partial access in testing, pointing to caching/session/token issues as part of the symptom set.

title: COMPANY LAPTOP
description: Device type used by two of the affected users to access SharePoint. Device context reported to help rule out client-side VPN or device-specific blockers (VPN not required; cloud-only environment).

title: PRIVATE DEVICE
description: Device type used by one affected user (a private device) to access SharePoint; behavior compared to company laptops during troubleshooting.

title: AZURE AD / ENTRA ID
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: AZURE AD / ENTRA ID is the tenant’s identity provider (Azure Active Directory / Entra ID) that manages user and group objects, issues authentication tokens and claims, evaluates Conditional Access policies (including use of device compliance state as an input), and records sign-in events and token issuance in its sign-in logs. The service hosts the Azure AD groups COMPLIANCE_TEAM, COMPLIANCE_CORE, and COMPLIANCE_EXT, and the sign-in events, token issuance records, and Conditional Access evaluations referenced in the analysis originate from this Entra ID/Azure AD tenant.

title: DOCUMENT LIBRARY
description: The SharePoint document library hosting the Projects folder structure that contains /Projects/Compliance_Review_2025. Investigated to ensure it was fully loaded and to confirm folder-level unique permissions and inheritance behavior.

title: INCOGNITO MODE
description: Browser private/incognito mode used during testing (Edge Incognito). Tests in Incognito showed different/partial access behavior compared to a normal browser session, indicating caching/session/token differences affecting access to the folder.

title: SHAREPOINT SENSITIVITY LABEL ACCESS ISSUE
description: Support ticket recording investigation and remediation of inconsistent access to a SharePoint folder caused by a Sensitivity Label encryption applied after unique permissions were set. Timeline: analysis of Azure AD sign‑in logs (24.03.25), in‑depth review via Microsoft Purview / Information Protection (24.03.25), reproduction in a test tenant (24.03.25), root cause defined (25.03.25), remediation actions performed (25.03.25), monitoring and customer confirmation (26–28.03.25), ticket closed 28.03.25 16:02. Customer contact: Contact Person 02. Resolver: Consulting Sample Company.

title: SENSITIVITY LABEL
description: Microsoft Information Protection / AIP sensitivity label applied to the SharePoint folder that performed encryption and associated key/per‑group permission mappings. Important attribute: when applied, the "Preserve custom permissions" option was not activated, so encryption references different permission groups than the SharePoint ACL until corrected.

title: ACCESS TOKENS
description: User access tokens (cached/older tokens) issued by Azure AD that contained group/claim information. Token caching/refresh behavior caused inconsistent access: users with older tokens could temporarily access while access was blocked after token renewal because encryption referenced a different permission group than the SharePoint ACL.

title: SHAREPOINT UNIQUE PERMISSIONS / ACL
description: The SharePoint folder's access control list (unique permissions) that granted access to a particular group (renamed/assigned before encryption). Because the Sensitivity Label encryption referenced a different group, the ACL and encryption key permissions diverged causing inconsistent access.

title: AUDIT TEAM
description: Customer's audit or compliance team that validated and confirmed stable access for employees after remediation and testing.

title: FOLDER (SHAREPOINT FOLDER)
description: The specific SharePoint folder that was renamed, assigned Unique Permissions (ACL) and subsequently encrypted with a Sensitivity Label. The folder's permission change sequence (rename → unique ACL → label applied) created the misalignment between SharePoint ACLs and label/encryption group references.

title: TOKEN CACHING
description: Token caching (cached/older access tokens) behavior in Azure AD/Azure AD clients that caused inconsistent access: older cached tokens containing prior group claims permitted temporary access until tokens were refreshed/renewed, at which point the encryption group mismatch caused access to be blocked.

title: PRESERVE CUSTOM PERMISSIONS OPTION
description: The Sensitivity Label option "Preserve custom permissions" that, when enabled, preserves or maps existing SharePoint custom/unique permissions during label application. In this case the option was not activated, so the label's encryption permissions did not preserve the folder's existing ACL mapping.

title: TI-1548823
description: Support incident opened 14.01.25 (KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox / sporadic SMTP error). Created via email by Contact Person 08 for Customer Company 22 AG. Priority High. Assigned to Employee 34 then escalated to System Engineer 07 / BU Infrastructure Engineering. Activities include client troubleshooting, Exchange Admin Center checks, log analysis, selective re-enabling of SMTP AUTH and Conditional Access adjustments. Closed 22.01.25 with root cause and remediation documented.

title: CUSTOMER COMPANY 22 AG
description: Customer organization reporting the incident; approximately 12 Sales team users experienced outbound email failures with locally installed Outlook clients. The ticket was opened on behalf of this organization and remediation was performed for its tenant/environment.

title: CONTACT PERSON 08
description: CONTACT PERSON 08 is the primary contact at Customer Company 22 AG who opened support tickets by email and reported two separate incidents: (1) emails stuck in the Outbox with error 0x800CCC0E indicating the SMTP server was not responding, and (2) a new employee unable to see any printers in the system. They provided initial symptom details for both issues and confirmed after remediation that email sending was restored and the printer became visible.

title: EMPLOYEE 34
description: EMPLOYEE 34 was the helpdesk engineer initially assigned to ticket TI-1548823 and subsequently acted as the support owner for the issue. They performed client‑side troubleshooting—recreated the Outlook profile, removed and re‑added the account, verified SMTP settings (port 587 / STARTTLS), disabled antivirus SMTP scanning, and checked the client firewall—then contacted the customer. When those initial remediation steps did not resolve the problem, EMPLOYEE 34 escalated to System Engineer 07. As the support owner they carried out further analysis and tests (one‑page and 25‑page scans), reviewed mail and SMTP relay logs, coordinated with the mail team, and adjusted the multifunction scan device settings (tested reduced scan resolution and enabled PDF compression). After verifying successful deliveries, EMPLOYEE 34 closed the ticket.

title: EXCHANGE ONLINE
description: Microsoft 365 hosted Exchange service (Exchange Online) forming part of the customer's mail environment. OWA/webmail access worked for users, indicating cloud mailboxes and inbound/online sending were functional from the service side.

title: ON-PREM EXCHANGE SERVER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: On-premises Exchange server(s) present in a hybrid configuration with Exchange Online; logs and Event Log entries on the on-prem servers were examined (including SMTP AUTH failure events) and Send/Hybrid connectors and TLS certificates were checked as part of the analysis.

title: HYBRID CONNECTOR
description: Hybrid connector / configuration responsible for mail routing between on-premises Exchange and Exchange Online (Send Connector / Hybrid Send path). Suspected initially and investigated as a possible source of outbound SMTP/authentication issues.

title: SEND CONNECTOR
description: Exchange send connector(s) on-premises used to route outbound mail to Exchange Online or external recipients; checked by engineers as part of hybrid setup analysis.

title: SMTP AUTH
description: SMTP Authentication mechanism used by legacy/desktop mail clients to authenticate to SMTP servers. Identified as disabled by tenant security settings (Security Defaults enabled), causing legacy Outlook clients (Build 2019 MSI) to fail authentication with "535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful" and leading to emails remaining in Outbox.

title: SECURITY DEFAULT POLICY
description: Microsoft tenant-level security defaults setting that was recently enabled for the customer tenant; its activation resulted in SMTP AUTH being disabled for legacy clients, which was the root cause of the sending failures for older Outlook clients.

title: CONDITIONAL ACCESS POLICY
description: Conditional Access configuration adjusted by the support team to selectively allow SMTP AUTH for affected users/clients. The CA policy change was part of the remediation to re-enable legacy SMTP authentication for the Sales team while maintaining overall tenant security posture.

title: OUTLOOK (LEGACY BUILD 2019 MSI)
description: Locally installed Outlook clients used by affected users (Windows Outlook MSI build 2019). These legacy clients relied on SMTP AUTH and were unable to send mail when SMTP AUTH was disabled by tenant security defaults; client-side troubleshooting steps were performed against these clients.

title: WINDOWS 11 WORKSTATION
description: WINDOWS 11 WORKSTATION: Customers' user endpoints running Windows 11 with locally installed Outlook, where an email sending failure manifested. On these workstations client-side troubleshooting was performed (firewall checks, antivirus SMTP scanning checks and user profile recreation) to isolate the problem. The affected operating system environment also had privacy settings that controlled camera access and were configured to block camera usage by apps, including Microsoft Teams and web browsers.

title: OWA (WEBMAIL)
description: Outlook Web Access / Exchange Online webmail. Verified working for affected users, indicating that mailbox and server-side delivery/sending via web auth were functioning and the issue was specific to legacy SMTP-authenticating clients.

title: IOS MOBILE DEVICES
description: Mobile devices (iOS) used by some users which were reported to be able to send mail successfully, further isolating the problem to legacy desktop clients using SMTP AUTH.

title: SYSTEM ENGINEER 07
description: Escalated specialist (Exchange/System Engineer) assigned to the ticket after initial triage. Performed hybrid setup analysis, checked Send Connector and TLS certificates, reviewed Event Log entries, identified SMTP AUTH disabled and Security Defaults enabled as root cause, and implemented/selectively enabled SMTP AUTH plus adjusted Conditional Access policy.

title: BU INFRASTRUCTURE ENGINEERING
description: Internal escalation team receiving the ticket for deeper infrastructure analysis (Exchange specialists, hybrid and mailflow owners). System Engineer 07 is assigned from this business unit.

title: EXCHANGE ADMIN CENTER
description: Management UI for Exchange / Microsoft 365 used during analysis to check mailflow (Queue Monitoring) and run Message Trace; used to confirm no global outage and to inspect queues/traces as part of diagnosis.

title: MESSAGE TRACE
description: Exchange/Exchange Online message tracing facility used to analyze mailflow and check for blocking or delivery issues; Message Trace revealed no external blocking, supporting the diagnosis that clients failed at SMTP auth.

title: QUEUE MONITORING
description: Mail queue monitoring on Exchange servers used to inspect mail queues and identify message delivery stalls as part of the mailflow analysis.

title: EVENT LOG
description: On-premises Exchange server Event Log entries examined during troubleshooting; contained SMTP authentication failure events (e.g., "535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful") that directly indicated SMTP AUTH failures for legacy clients.

title: SPF / DKIM
description: Email authentication records (SPF and DKIM) noted as unchanged during the analysis; checked to rule out external delivery/anti-spam rejection causes.

title: SMTP SERVER
description: Referenced conceptually in the error ("SMTP server not responding") and represented by the exchange mail servers (on-prem or cloud endpoints) the legacy Outlook clients attempted to contact using SMTP AUTH.

title: MAILFLOW MONITORING
description: Ongoing monitoring performed after remediation (24-hour mailflow observation) to confirm no further SMTP errors; used to validate the fix before final closure.

title: CLIENT FIREWALL
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Local workstation firewall on affected Windows 11 endpoints that was checked as part of client-side troubleshooting to ensure it was not blocking outbound SMTP connections from Outlook.

title: ANTIVIRUS SMTP SCANNING
description: Antivirus/endpoint security feature on user workstations that inspects SMTP traffic; it was disabled during testing to rule out interference with Outlook's SMTP submission.

title: ERROR 0X800CCC0E
description: Client-side Outlook error code reported by Contact Person 08 ("0x800CCC0E – Connection to the server failed / SMTP server not responding") indicating the client failed to establish or authenticate an SMTP session; used as a diagnostic symptom.

title: ERROR 535 5.7.3 AUTHENTICATION UNSUCCESSFUL
description: SMTP authentication failure event observed in the on-prem Exchange Event Log ("535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful"), providing server-side evidence that legacy clients were failing SMTP AUTH attempts.

title: OUTLOOK PROFILE RECREATION
description: Client configuration action taken during troubleshooting (recreated Outlook profile and removed/re-added account) to rule out corrupt local profile or account configuration as the cause of sending failures.

title: EXCHANGE HYBRID (ON-PREM + M365)
description: Describes the customer's hybrid mail architecture combining on-premises Exchange servers and Exchange Online (M365). This environment framing drove investigation steps (Send Connector, TLS certs, hybrid connector, routing) during the incident analysis.

title: MAIL QUEUES
description: On-premises Exchange mail queues inspected via Queue Monitoring as part of mailflow analysis to detect stuck/queued messages or delivery bottlenecks during the incident.

title: LEGACY AUTHENTICATION
description: Conceptual category of older auth methods used by legacy clients (e.g., SMTP AUTH); affected by tenant security configuration ("Security Defaults") and managed via Conditional Access adjustments to selectively permit older authentication flows.

title: TI-1394201
description:
TI-1394201 (KF14: Question FileZilla Server @ KF14) is a Customer Company 14 AG support ticket opened on 29.11.2024 by Admin, CRM / Employee 16 (contact: Contact Person 16) via email with priority Normal. The ticket, ultimately owned by Employee 26, was initially assigned to BU Customer Services Run, then to Employee 27, set In Progress on 02.12.2024, transferred to Employee 26 and closed on 13.12.2024 with the closure note "Ticket can be closed."

The ticket documents inquiries about who installed FileZilla Server on a virtual server at the KF14 site and reports of FTP/FTP(S) access problems. Troubleshooting activities recorded include workstation FTP/SFTP login tests, firewall analysis, and verification of FileZilla site settings. As part of remediation, external FTP access was later decommissioned. The lifecycle notes indicate troubleshooting was performed and the issue was resolved to the handlers’ satisfaction before closure.

title: CUSTOMER COMPANY 14 AG
description: CUSTOMER COMPANY 14 AG is the customer organization for which ticket TI-1394201 was opened. The company reported an issue concerning a FileZilla Server installation on its infrastructure — specifically a virtual server located at the customer site/context identified as KF14 — and the ticket was raised on behalf of CUSTOMER COMPANY 14 AG. Contact Person 16 is the designated representative for the company in that ticket.

title: FILEZILLA SERVER
description: FILEZILLA SERVER: The FileZilla Server FTP application is installed on the virtual server KF14 and is the component implicated in a customer ticket reporting FTP access problems. The customer has asked who installed or configured the service. The issue was investigated by the Core Team and support staff, who reviewed the site settings and FTP configuration settings as part of troubleshooting.

title: VIRTUAL SERVER AT KF14
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: "VIRTUAL SERVER AT KF14" is the virtual machine/hosting environment at site KF14 where FileZilla Server was installed. It is the VM referenced in the ticket subject ("on the virtual server at KF14") and is the physical/logical location of the FTP service. As such, this host represents the hosting context and is the focal point for the reported access issues and any troubleshooting, configuration or operational actions.

title: FTP (FILE TRANSFER PROTOCOL)
description: The network/file-transfer service provided by the FileZilla Server (protocol/service facing the reported access problems). The ticket is about FTP access difficulties to the FileZilla Server.

title: KF14 (SITE/ENVIRONMENT)
description: Identifier for the site/environment referenced in the ticket title and the location of the virtual server (KF14). Represents the customer site or environment where the virtual server and FileZilla Server reside.

title: PORTAL – CONSULTINGSAMPLECOMPANY
description: Consulting Sample Company's ticketing/portal system referenced in the ticket notification ("You can view the current processing status ... in the Consulting Sample Company Portal"). Used to record status, transfers, and ticket metadata.

title: RAMEZ
description: Named individual referenced in an automated message greeting ("Hello Ramez") — likely an internal staff member or recipient associated with the ticket workflow (message recipient when status changed to In Progress).

title: NO-REPLY (SYSTEM ACCOUNT)
description: Automated/system sender account appearing in ticket system messages (annotated as "NO-REPLY" in assignment/notification lines). Used by the ticketing system to publish automated assignment and status-change messages (e.g., "Ticket assigned ... #001063825 NO-REPLY").

title: CONTACTPERSON16@CUSTOMERCOMPANY14.EXAMPLE
description: Email address of the reporting contact (Contact Person 16). Appears in the original message headers and was used to send the request to Consulting Sample Company helpdesk; represents the customer's email endpoint used in the support exchange.
