description: Domain name and web endpoint for Consulting Sample Company (example: www.ConsultingSampleCompany.com/ConsultingSampleCompany). Hosts the customer support portal referenced in ticket notifications ("You can view the current processing status ... in the Consulting Sample Company Portal") and resolves portal/service endpoints used for ticket management and notifications.

title: EMPLOYEE 28
description: Internal engineer who performed the decommissioning of external FTP access and removed the related firewall policy on 17.12.24 (screenshot and change noted).

title: APPLICATIONUSER, CRM DEVELOPMENT
description: Automated/system account entry ("ApplicationUser, CRM Development WEB") that scheduled the ticket for 02.12; represents a system/service actor recorded by the ticketing system.

title: KF14AZEDI01
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Named virtual server at the KF14 site ("Server: KF14azedi01") identified in troubleshooting notes. Hosts the FileZilla Server instance and is associated with the FTP service IP 10.10.10.10 on port 21.

title: FTP
description: File Transfer Protocol service in use for external/internal file transfers; login tests were performed against FTP and SFTP endpoints to validate access. FileZilla Server provides FTP service on the server.

title: SFTP
description: Secure FTP protocol used as part of troubleshooting (login tests performed on FTP/SFTP); indicates alternative/secure transfer method considered during diagnostics.

title: WINSCP
description: Client application used during troubleshooting to perform login tests against the FTP/SFTP service from the customer workstation.

type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: IP address and port noted for the FTP service (10.10.10.10 Port 21). Represents the network endpoint used for FTP access tests and subject to firewall analysis.

title: CT_001680
description: Username reported in troubleshooting notes ("UserName: Ct_001680") used for FTP login tests. Likely a service or customer account used to authenticate to the FTP service.

title: FIREWALL POLICY
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Specific firewall policy/ruleset that controlled external FTP access for the KF14 site; a firewall policy removal is recorded as part of the decommissioning on 17.12.24.

title: CORE TEAM
description: Internal technical/core team consulted for checking FTP settings and FileZilla site settings; collaborated with support engineer during further analyses.

title: EXTERNAL FTP ACCESS
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: The externally-accessible FTP service footprint for the customer/site; specifically noted as being decommissioned on 17.12.24 when the firewall policy was removed.

title: CUSTOMER WORKSTATION
description: Customer-side workstation used during troubleshooting. Support performed login tests from this machine, checked FTP client settings and Device-level behavior during diagnostics.

title: CRM WEB
description: The CRM/ticketing web application used to create, assign and manage TI-1394201 (records show "Admin, CRM WEB" and "ApplicationUser, CRM Development WEB" performing actions). Hosts ticket lifecycle operations, assignments and scheduling.

title: CONSULTING SAMPLE COMPANY WEBSITE
description: Public/support web presence referenced in the ticket signature (www.ConsultingSampleCompany.com/ConsultingSampleCompany). Provides ticket/customer portal, survey links and company contact information related to the support process.

title: SURVEY PORTAL
description: Customer feedback/survey link referenced in the closure text ("To the survey") used to collect brief feedback on completed tickets; part of the consulting company's customer portal/workflow.

title: KF14 (SITE)
description: Site/location identifier for the customer environment referenced throughout the ticket (context for "KF14azedi01", "virtual server at KF14", and "KF14: Question FileZilla Server @ KF14"). Represents the specific customer site/tenant location under Customer Company 14 AG.

title: TI-722423
description: Support incident opened 27.03.24 12:04 (Ticket name: KF11: Intune NDES Connector lost communication). Origin: Phone. Owner: Employee 11. Contact person: Contact Person 8. Created for Customer Company 11. Status: Closed 14.06.24 with closure text "Incident successfully resolved". Activities: troubleshooting email trail describing firewall rule adjustments, connector reinstallation without proxy, temporary MFA exclusion for a service account, role reassignment actions, and attached logs/blueprint references.

title: CONTACT PERSON 8
description: Named contact at Customer Company 11 and primary contact referenced in the ticket activities/emails; recipient of Employee 11's status/update email about remediation steps.

title: CONTACT PERSON 2
description: Additional contact at Customer Company 11 copied/mentioned in the email exchange about the Intune NDES Connector incident.

title: INTUNE NDES CONNECTOR
description: The Intune NDES (Network Device Enrollment Service) Connector that lost communication (core subject of the incident). It was reinstalled without proxy configuration, returned to "green" status in Azure indicating restored connectivity to the Intune service, and is used to support SCEP certificate profiles for Intune-managed devices. Logs were attached to validate correct operation.

title: AZURE
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Azure environment/portal where the NDES connector's status is monitored (connector shown as "green" in Azure after reinstallation), used to observe connector health and connectivity to Intune.

title: FIREWALL RULES
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: FIREWALL RULES: Network firewall rules and NSG configurations (including host/Windows Firewall, perimeter firewall, and Azure NSG rules) that control outbound and internal connectivity. Employee 11, working with the network team, adjusted these rules as part of remediation to restore communication between the NDES connector and the Intune service. These rules must explicitly permit outbound HTTPS (TCP 443), DNS resolution, and required internal Active Directory ports; incomplete or changed rules are a frequent root cause of sensors reporting an “unhealthy” state.

title: PROXY CONFIGURATION
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Proxy settings/configuration that had been in use for the connector; the connector was reinstalled without proxy configuration as part of the fix, implying the proxy had been implicated in the communication problem.

title: INTUNE ADMIN ROLE
description: The Intune Admin role (Azure/Intune RBAC role) that was temporarily assigned to the service account to allow reinstallation/operation of the connector and removed again afterwards; must be re-granted if a reinstall is required.

title: WPS TEAM
description: WPS team referenced as an alternate contact for issues during Intune deployment (advice in the closure message to contact the WPS team if issues occur).

title: MICROSOFT GUIDE – CONFIGURE INFRASTRUCTURE TO SUPPORT SCEP CERTIFICATE PROFILES WITH MICROSOFT INTUNE
description: Official Microsoft guide referenced and followed during the connector installation ("Configure infrastructure to support SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune"); used as the installation/configuration source of truth for SCEP/NDES integration with Intune.

title: BLUEPRINT_1
description: Internal or referenced blueprint/log bundle ("blueprint_1") mentioned in the email as an attachment or reference to quickly verify connector operation; used alongside attached logs for validation.

title: LOGS
description: Operational logs attached to the ticket/email provided to quickly verify whether the connector is operating correctly after reinstallation; used for validation and troubleshooting.

title: NDES (ON-PREM)
description: On-premises Network Device Enrollment Service (NDES) component of Active Directory Certificate Services that issues SCEP certificates to devices. The Intune NDES Connector integrates with on-prem NDES to enable SCEP certificate profile enrollment for Intune-managed devices.

title: SCEP CERTIFICATE PROFILES
description: SCEP (Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol) certificate profiles used by Microsoft Intune to provision device certificates via NDES; the Microsoft guide referenced is specifically about configuring infrastructure to support these SCEP profiles.

title: TI-1624682
description: Support incident opened 15.01.26 (Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail fails for larger documents). Source: Email. Contact: Contact Person 28. Owner: Employee 34. Timeline: created 15.01.26, analysis and tests performed 15–23.01.26, closure 23.01.26. Closure note: "SMTP and mail size limit adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail stable." Records include mail-log review, device tests, coordination with mail team, and configuration changes per the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard.

title: CONTACT PERSON 28
description: Named customer contact who reported that larger scanned PDFs (~20–30 pages) were not delivered and provided initial symptom details (small scans OK, larger scans not delivered; printer reports "Send successful").

title: INTERNAL MAIL SERVER
description: Recipient/internal mail infrastructure component; internal mail server logs showed no incoming message for large files (i.e., scans were blocked at the SMTP relay and never reached internal mailbox server).

title: PRINTER (MFP) / SCAN DEVICE
description: Multi‑function device used for scanning and sending via Scan-to-Mail. Device reported "Send successful" even when messages were blocked by SMTP relay. Device had default scan settings of 600 dpi and PDF compression disabled, producing large PDFs for multi‑page scans.

title: SCAN-TO-MAIL SERVICE
description: The print device feature/workflow that sends scanned documents by email via the dedicated SMTP relay. Dependent on scan profile (resolution, compression) and on mail infrastructure size limits; was failing for larger documents until scan settings and mail limits were adjusted.

title: MAIL LOGS
description: Logs reviewed during troubleshooting: internal mail server logs (no incoming message for large files) and SMTP relay logs (explicit "Message size exceeds fixed limit"), which provided the primary diagnostic evidence that scans were being rejected due to size constraints.

title: MAIL SIZE LIMIT (10 MB)
description: Configured maximum message size enforced by the SMTP relay after a recent policy change; documented as 10 MB during investigation. This limit caused multi‑page, high‑resolution PDF scans to be rejected until the limit was moderately increased and/or scan sizes reduced.

title: SCAN PROFILE / DEVICE CONFIGURATION
description: Scan device configuration controlling resolution (default 600 dpi), PDF compression (disabled initially), and profile used for Scan-to-Mail. Adjustments made during remediation: default resolution reduced to 300 dpi and PDF compression enabled, reducing file sizes and allowing successful delivery alongside a moderate increase of the SMTP limit.

title: RESOLUTION 600 DPI
description: Default scan resolution configured on the device (600 dpi) that produced large multi‑page PDFs; identified as a contributor to oversized messages during the investigation.

title: RESOLUTION 300 DPI
description: Temporary reduced scan resolution used during testing (300 dpi) which produced smaller PDFs and successfully delivered a 25‑page test scan, confirming that resolution affects message size.

title: SEND SUCCESSFUL MESSAGE
description: Status/confirmation message displayed by the printer ("Send successful") after initiating email delivery; misleading in this case because the SMTP relay later rejected oversized messages so recipients did not receive them.

title: SMTP RELAY LOG ENTRY: \"MESSAGE SIZE EXCEEDS FIXED LIMIT\"
description: Specific SMTP relay log entry observed during mail-log review that indicated rejected scan messages due to exceeding the configured maximum message size; this log entry was the primary indicator of relay-level rejection.

title: MAXIMUM EMAIL SIZE CHANGE (REDUCTION TO LIMIT SPAM)
description: Recent policy/configuration change made ~two weeks prior that reduced the SMTP relay maximum message size to limit spam (documented as 10 MB during investigation); this change caused Scan-to-Mail deliveries of larger PDFs to be rejected until adjusted.

title: TI-1616128
description: Support incident opened 14.10.25 (Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Call participants cannot hear the user). Created via telephone at 09:18, assigned to Employee 33, closed 14.10.25 09:52. Report: user can hear others in Teams calls but other participants cannot hear the user; both internal and external calls affected. Closure: microphone not properly connected — device configured correctly and function restored. Activities include telephone report, helpdesk inquiry, remote session troubleshooting, and user verification.

title: USB HEADSET
description: The physical USB headset used by the user during Teams calls. Identified as the input device in Teams ("USB Audio Device"). The headset's microphone cable was not fully inserted, causing no audio input until reconnected.

title: MICROPHONE
description: The microphone element used for voice input during Teams calls (part of the USB headset). Showed no input activity in Teams and Windows until the headset microphone cable was properly connected.

title: MICROPHONE CABLE
description: Physical connection/cable on the headset that carries the microphone signal. Found not fully inserted which prevented any input signal from being detected; reseating restored audio.

title: USB AUDIO DEVICE
description: Device label/selection shown in Teams as the chosen microphone input ("USB Audio Device"). Selected in Teams but initially produced no input level until hardware connection was fixed.

title: WINDOWS SOUND MENU
description: Operating system audio control panel used during troubleshooting; showed no input signal for the selected device until the microphone cable was reconnected. Helped confirm absence/presence of input at the OS level.

title: CAMERA
description:
CAMERA: The entity is the physical (or integrated) camera hardware on the user’s workstation. The device is visible in Windows Device Manager (indicating drivers and hardware are present), but OS-level privacy/app-permission settings can block application access, preventing Teams and browsers from using the camera even though the driver is installed. In this incident the user reported the camera and chat worked during the same calls, confirming the camera hardware and drivers were functional and accessible in at least some contexts; the observed problem was therefore limited to audio input, not video. 

Implication: the camera exists and is generally operational, but intermittent or policy-driven privacy/permission settings (Windows camera privacy, browser site permissions, Teams device settings, or endpoint privacy controls) can cause apps to be denied camera access. For troubleshooting, verify Device Manager shows the camera enabled, check Windows Camera privacy settings and per-app permissions (Teams and browser), confirm Teams/browser site permissions, and review endpoint/AV or group policy privacy controls.

title: REMOTE SESSION
description: The remote support connection/session used by the support engineer (Employee 33) to connect to the affected employee's workstation for live troubleshooting and hardware verification. Enabled inspection of Teams settings and OS sound indicators.

title: TEAMS TEST CALL
description: Built-in Microsoft Teams diagnostic feature used to verify audio input/output during troubleshooting. Initially showed no recorded audio for the affected user and later confirmed successful microphone operation after reconnection.

title: TI-1571046
description: Support incident "KF25: SharePoint – Access works only partially after permission change" opened 04.03.25 via customer portal for Customer Company 25 AG. Contact: Contact Person 05. Owner/assignee: Employee 21. Symptoms: users added to project "ERP-Upgrade" generally have SharePoint access but the folder /Projects/ERP_Upgrade/Finance is not displayed for some users; intermittent success after logout/login. Key findings and closure: root cause identified as token caching combined with Conditional Access evaluation delay; Conditional Access policy adjusted and users re-authenticated; access stable after policy adjustment. Ticket closed 11.03.25 with closure text noting root cause and resolution.

title: CONTACT PERSON 05
description: Named contact at Customer Company 25 AG who reported the issue via the portal and received closure communication.

title: AZURE AD GROUP ERP_FINANCE
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Azure AD security/group "ERP_Finance" whose members were the affected users; this group was a member of the larger "SP_ERP_Project" group. Group membership and nesting were reviewed as part of the analysis (permissions themselves were correctly configured).

title: AZURE AD GROUP SP_ERP_PROJECT
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Azure AD security/group "SP_ERP_Project" representing the SharePoint project group; the "ERP_Finance" group is a member of this group. SharePoint permission assignments referenced this group structure.

title: CONDITIONAL ACCESS POLICY – REQUIRE COMPLIANT DEVICE FOR FINANCE RESOURCES
description: Tenant Conditional Access policy titled/characterized as "Require compliant device for Finance Resources" (policy adjusted on 28.02). The policy affected access to finance resources (including the SharePoint project folder) and evaluated device compliance status; this policy’s evaluation delay contributed to access problems for some users and was adjusted (folder excluded from the Finance-specific CA rule) as a remediation.

title: AUTHENTICATION TOKENS (CACHED TOKENS)
description: Issued authentication tokens used to access SharePoint. Analysis found tokens issued before users’ group membership changed (contain outdated claims) and were cached by clients; users who had not fully logged out kept using stale tokens and thus did not see updated access rights. Token caching and token issuance timing were central to the root cause.

title: AZURE SIGN-IN LOGS
description: Azure sign-in log records reviewed during analysis. Logs showed multiple entries with "Conditional Access not satisfied" alongside entries showing successful token issuance for the SharePoint root resource — evidence used to correlate CA evaluation failures, token issuance timing, and subsequent successful access after re-authentication.

title: HYBRID AZURE AD JOINED DEVICES
description: Clients that are hybrid Azure AD joined (on-prem AD + Azure AD). Two users who repeatedly experienced the issue were using hybrid-joined devices where device synchronization to Entra ID was faulty/delayed; device compliance status remained "unknown" or stale for 6–8 hours, delaying Conditional Access evaluation and contributing to access failures.

title: DEVICE SYNCHRONIZATION
description: Process/mechanism that synchronizes device state/compliance from endpoints/on-premises to Entra ID. In this case synchronization for some hybrid-joined devices was faulty or delayed (6–8 hours), and manual device sync was triggered as part of remediation to update device compliance state faster.

title: PRIVATE DEVICES
description: Personal/private devices used by some users to access corporate resources. These devices may not be managed/compliant and were explicitly noted as a source of access variability ("Some users access from private devices"), contributing to Conditional Access evaluation failures when device compliance is required.

title: OFFICE CLIENTS (OFFICE APPS)
description: Desktop Office applications (Office client sessions) referenced by the remediation step "User fully logged out (Office + browser)". Office client sessions can hold cached authentication tokens and require sign-out to force re-authentication and fresh token issuance.

title: BROWSER
description: Web browsers used to access SharePoint and other Office 365 services. Browsers maintain session tokens and caches; clearing browser cache and signing out was part of the remediation to remove stale/cached tokens and allow updated claims to be issued.

title: DEVICE COMPLIANCE STATUS
description: The reported compliance state of a device as recorded in Entra ID (e.g., "Compliant", "Not compliant", "Unknown"). The ticket documents device compliance as "partially 'unknown'" for some devices; stale/unknown compliance status caused Conditional Access to evaluate as "not satisfied" until device sync/updates occurred.

title: /PROJECTS/ERP_UPGRADE/FINANCE
description: The specific SharePoint folder/resource that was not displayed for affected users (/Projects/ERP_Upgrade/Finance). Permissions on SharePoint were confirmed correct; visibility problems were caused by token caching and Conditional Access evaluation timing rather than folder permission misconfiguration.

title: TI-1621089
description: Support incident opened 22.10.25 (Ticket name: KF60: Printer not visible in system). Reported via email by Contact Person 08 on behalf of Customer Company 60 AG. Assigned to Employee 15. Issue: a new employee could not see any printer in the system while colleagues in same office could. Analysis found a Group Policy (GPO) for printer assignment was not applied. Remediation: gpupdate /force executed and user re-logged in; printer was automatically mapped and test print succeeded. Ticket closed 22.10.25 with closure note: "GPO not applied. Group policies updated. Printer visible again."

title: GPO FOR PRINTER ASSIGNMENT
description: The Group Policy Object responsible for assigning/mapping location-based printers to users. It had not been processed/applied for the affected user, which prevented automatic printer mapping. The GPO was updated/applied (gpupdate /force) as the remediation.

title: USER ACCOUNT (AFFECTED NEW EMPLOYEE)
description: The end-user account for the new employee who could not see the printer. Account membership was checked and confirmed to be in the correct AD group for location-based printers; after forcing Group Policy update and re-login the account received the printer mapping and could print successfully.

title: AD GROUP FOR LOCATION-BASED PRINTERS
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Active Directory group used to control assignment of location-based printers via Group Policy. The affected user was a member of this AD group (membership was verified), and GPO processing uses group membership to map printers.

title: ACTIVE DIRECTORY DOMAIN CONTROLLER & MICROSOFT DEFENDER FOR IDENTITY STANDARD V1.0
description: Architecture and operational standard document dated 10.10.2024 describing required architecture, security requirements and operational procedures for Domain Controllers with Microsoft Defender for Identity Sensor in Customer Company 10. Objectives include stable AD monitoring, correct sensor operation, required network/firewall permissions, and standardized configuration across on‑prem and Azure DCs. Contains architecture overview, components list, network/port requirements, operational principles, decision tree and troubleshooting/use cases.

title: DOMAIN CONTROLLER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Active Directory domain controllers (on‑prem and Azure) that host Active Directory services and have the Defender for Identity sensor installed. Responsibilities include handling authentication (Kerberos/NTLM), LDAP and replication traffic; must maintain internal AD ports and consistent agent installation and health monitoring.

title: MICROSOFT DEFENDER FOR IDENTITY SENSOR
description: Agent software installed directly on Domain Controllers that captures and analyzes authentication processes, Kerberos and NTLM traffic, LDAP communication and replication behavior. Produces telemetry for security analysis; health depends on proper local firewall, perimeter/network egress, DNS resolution and connectivity to Defender cloud endpoints. Parallel installation of multiple sensors per DC is prohibited; agent version, service running state and reinstall procedures are noted in operational guidance.

title: MICROSOFT DEFENDER CLOUD SERVICE
description: Cloud service that receives telemetry from Defender for Identity sensors for centralized security analysis. Requires outbound connectivity (HTTPS/TCP 443) and DNS resolution to Microsoft endpoints; sensor inability to reach this service results in "unhealthy" sensor status, connection errors and missing security alerts.

title: WINDOWS FIREWALL (ON DC)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Host‑based firewall running on each Domain Controller that must permit sensor outbound connections and internal AD ports (LDAP, Kerberos, RPC). Misconfigured local firewall rules can block telemetry or internal authentication flows and cause sensor “unhealthy” status.

title: PERIMETER FIREWALL
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Network edge/firewall that must allow outbound HTTPS to Microsoft Defender endpoints and permit required inbound/internal AD ports. Changes or restrictive rules at the perimeter can block sensor telemetry and cause multiple DCs to report issues.

title: AZURE NSG
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Azure Network Security Group used for Azure‑hosted Domain Controllers that controls network traffic (including outbound egress). NSG rules blocking outbound 443 or Microsoft endpoints can prevent sensors on Azure DCs from reaching Defender cloud services and mark them unhealthy.

title: OUTBOUND HTTPS (TCP 443)
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Required network egress protocol/port for Defender sensors to communicate with Microsoft Defender cloud endpoints. Document lists this as a mandatory outbound connectivity requirement; blocked/filtered outbound HTTPS results in sensor connectivity failures.

title: INTERNAL AD PORTS (LDAP, KERBEROS, RPC)
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Core internal Active Directory ports and protocols that must remain available and unchanged for AD functionality and for sensors to accurately monitor authentication/replication traffic. Blocking or modifying these ports breaks AD operations and sensor analysis.

title: PROXY / PROXY AUTHENTICATION
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Proxy infrastructure or proxy authentication mechanisms that, if present or changed, can block or interfere with sensor outbound HTTPS connections or require additional configuration (proxy credentials, exceptions). Document recommends checking proxy authentication as a troubleshooting step.

title: TLS / CERTIFICATE ISSUES
description: TLS and certificate‑related problems (expired/invalid certificates, TLS policy changes) that can prevent secure HTTPS connections between sensors and Defender cloud endpoints or otherwise cause telemetry and connectivity failures. Listed as a troubleshooting item if sensors oscillate healthy/unhealthy.

title: DEFENDER SERVICE HEALTH PORTAL
description: Microsoft service health portal referenced as an operational check for Defender service availability. Document recommends consulting the portal as part of troubleshooting to determine whether service issues are on Microsoft’s side.

title: NETWORK SEGMENT / TOPOLOGY CHANGES
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Changes to network segments or topology that can alter routing, firewall/NSG scope or reachability of Microsoft endpoints. Document requires review of network segment changes as they may impact sensor connectivity and AD telemetry.

title: SENSOR SERVICE (RUNNING/REINSTALL)
description: Operational state of the Defender Sensor service on a DC (service running, stopped, or requiring reinstall). Document guidance: verify service running, monitor health after reinstall, and do not install multiple sensors in parallel on one DC.

title: DECISION TREE & TROUBLESHOOTING CHECKS
description: Structured troubleshooting flow within the document covering single‑DC vs multiple‑DC issues, checks for service status, outbound connectivity, DNS resolution, event logs, firewall/NSG changes, proxy/TLS issues, agent version and further steps including reinstall and consulting Defender Service Health Portal.

title: ON-PREMISES DOMAIN CONTROLLER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Domain Controllers located in the customer's on‑premises environment. Host Active Directory services and Defender for Identity Sensor; rely on host Windows Firewall and on‑prem perimeter rules for outbound connectivity and must preserve internal AD ports (LDAP, Kerberos, RPC).

title: AZURE DOMAIN CONTROLLER
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Domain Controllers hosted in Microsoft Azure (Azure VMs) that run Active Directory and the Defender for Identity Sensor; network security is governed by Azure NSGs and cloud networking rules, and they require outbound HTTPS, DNS resolution and correct NSG configuration to reach Defender cloud endpoints.

title: EVENT LOGS
description: Local and sensor event logs (Sensor / network errors) on Domain Controllers and Defender sensors that capture authentication failures, network errors and other diagnostic data used in troubleshooting per the decision tree. These logs are an evidence source for detecting unhealthy sensor states and root‑cause analysis.

title: HEALTH ALERTS
description: Security and health alerts produced by the Defender for Identity sensors and surfaced in the Defender Cloud Service (and health portal). Missing or absent alerts indicate telemetry/connectivity problems and are a primary symptom noted in the document when sensors report "unhealthy".

title: INTERNAL SECURITY SOFTWARE
description: On‑host or network security software (e.g., endpoint protection, local intrusion prevention, network appliances) operating inside the customer environment that can block telemetry or network connections from the Defender sensor to cloud endpoints and is listed as a potential cause to check in troubleshooting.

title: PRINT & SCANNING INFRASTRUCTURE STANDARD – CUSTOMER COMPANY
description: Standard document (Version 1.0, 15.10.2025) authored by IT Infrastructure and approved; defines the architecture, design principles, operational procedures and decision tree for centrally managed print and scan services for the customer company. Core principles: central printer management via Windows print servers, automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO), standardized driver versions, location-based printer concepts, uniform Scan-to-Mail configuration. Includes provisioning, driver compatibility checks, VLAN design, and troubleshooting decision tree for printing and scanning use cases.

title: CUSTOMER COMPANY
description: The customer organization for which the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard applies; the operational environment and locations referenced by the document.

title: IT INFRASTRUCTURE
description: Authoring IT team/department responsible for the print & scan standard, architecture, maintenance and enforcement of policies (driver approvals, GPO deployment, VLAN segmentation, Scan-to-Mail configuration).

title: WINDOWS PRINT SERVERS
type: SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE (APPLICATION SERVERS, DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, VIRTUAL MACHINES)
description: Centralized Windows-based print servers that host printer queues, manage driver deployments and act as the only network access point for printers from client devices. Core element of the infrastructure base described in the standard.

title: PRINTERS
description: Physical printers and networked printing devices (including multifunction devices) deployed in locations; configured as queues on the print server, mapped to users/groups, located in a dedicated printer VLAN, and provided with standardized drivers and location/default settings.

title: MULTIFUNCTION PRINTER
description: Subset of printers with scanning capabilities used for Scan-to-Mail; typically located per-location, configured as default or multifunction devices and integrated with the SMTP relay using a dedicated service account and TLS.

title: LOCATION-BASED PRINTER QUEUES
description: Printer queue constructs created on Windows print servers and assigned to users/devices via GPO based on location or department; used to enforce location-specific defaults and provisioning.

title: POINT-AND-PRINT POLICY
description: Windows/Group Policy setting and security restriction that governs client driver installation behavior (Point-and-Print). The standard requires verification of Point-and-Print restrictions and compatibility after Windows updates to avoid driver or mapping issues.

title: DRIVER STANDARD
description: Organizational standard specifying approved and tested printer driver versions. Drivers are configured on print servers and must be validated after OS updates to ensure compatibility and correct printer mapping.

title: USER DEVICE (WORKSTATION)
description: End-user computers (Windows workstations) that initiate print and scan jobs; they receive printer mappings via GPO and submit jobs to the central print server. Device Manager on these devices is used to confirm local hardware visibility.

title: TLS CONFIGURATION
description: TLS-based transport security used to secure SMTP relay connections for Scan-to-Mail; the standard notes that TLS must be validated and changes to mail servers/authentication may affect scan functionality. (Mapped to certificate/TLS configuration management obligations in the environment.

title: AD GROUPS
type: IDENTITY PROVIDER / AUTHENTICATION SERVICE (ADFS, ENTRA ID)
description: Active Directory groups used to control printer assignment and access (assignment via AD groups), and to determine default printers per location/department as part of provisioning and access checks.

title: MAIL QUEUE
description: Message queue on the SMTP relay or mail server used to hold outgoing scan emails; troubleshooting step for "no delivery" scan issues (check mail queue and spam/filter rules).

title: SPAM / FILTER RULES
description: Mail system filtering and anti-spam rules that may prevent delivered scans from reaching their destination; included in the decision tree as a check when scanned mail is not delivered.

title: DEVICE MANAGER
description: DEVICE MANAGER: a Windows system utility on user devices used during troubleshooting to verify that local hardware and its drivers are present and recognized. Troubleshooters use Device Manager to confirm camera hardware and drivers are visible even when application-level access to the camera is blocked, and likewise to confirm local printers and scanners are detected (often as a decision-tree step alongside the Windows "Printers & Scanners" settings). Because Device Manager reports device and driver presence independently of app permissions or privacy/policy blocks, technicians use it to distinguish hardware/driver faults from application permission or configuration issues and to determine whether next steps should focus on driver reinstallation, privacy/policy settings, or further Printers & Scanners troubleshooting.

title: SCAN-TO-MAIL
description: Service/process that delivers scanned documents via email from multifunction printers to recipients. Implemented using a dedicated SMTP relay, authenticated by a configured service account and protected by TLS; delivery depends on SMTP relay reachability, authentication, mail queue processing and spam/filter rules.

title: PRINT JOB
description: Individual print task submitted by a user device to a printer queue on the print server. Represented in the print queue, may become stuck, paused, or fail; its presence/absence in the queue is used to determine client-side vs server-side faults.

title: PAUSED PRINT QUEUE
description: Operational state of a print queue where job dispatching is intentionally or unintentionally suspended; decision tree instructs checking whether the queue is paused when jobs remain stuck.

title: DEFAULT PRINTER
description: Per-location or per-user default printer assignment applied automatically by the provisioning process and enforced via GPO/AD group membership; used to ensure consistent user experience and location-based defaults.

title: VLAN REACHABILITY / NETWORK PING
type: NETWORK INFRASTRUCTURE (FIREWALLS, VLANS, NAT, LOAD BALANCERS)
description: Network-level connectivity checks (ICMP ping, VLAN routing/ACL validation) used to verify client access to printers or print servers and to isolate network/VLAN-related print failures as described in the decision tree.

title: KF52: MICROSOFT TEAMS – CAMERA NOT DETECTED (TI-1615042)
description: Support incident opened 07.10.25 (Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Camera not detected). Ticket number TI-1615042. Company: Customer Company 52 AG. Contact: Contact Person 09. Owner: Employee 33. Origin: Email. Created 07.10.25 09:12. Closed 07.10.25 11:06. Closure: Camera restored in Teams; root cause was OS permission and blocked device setting; remediation performed according to referenced Blueprint.

title: CONTACT PERSON 09
description: Named contact at Customer Company 52 AG who reported the camera issue, provided OS details (Windows 11) and browser behavior, and received communications about the incident.

title: BLUEPRINT – TEAMS CLIENT DEVICES & PERMISSIONS – VERSION 1.2
description: Document referenced and followed during troubleshooting. Contains standard checks and remediation steps for Teams client device permissions (OS privacy settings, app permissions, Teams restart). Actions were executed according to this Blueprint to resolve the issue.

title: BROWSER (TEAMS WEB)
description: Web browser used to access Teams Web; camera was also unavailable in the browser, indicating the problem was at the OS/permission level rather than only the Teams desktop client.

title: OPERATING SYSTEM PERMISSIONS (CAMERA PRIVACY / APP PERMISSIONS)
description: Windows privacy and app permission settings that control whether applications (including Teams and browsers) may access the camera. These settings were disabled for apps and were adjusted (per the Blueprint) to allow camera access, restoring functionality.

title: EMAIL (TICKET ORIGIN)
description: Represents the email communication channel used to create/open the support ticket (ticket origin listed as Email). Indicates that the incident was reported and tracked via email messages between the customer and the support organization.
